info.name: Salesdock API
info.description:
## **Introduction**

The Salesdock API allows you to interact with an account in Salesdock. The API is RESTful and uses JSON to transport information.

The main use case for interacting with a Salsedock account through the API is to help in automated creation and retrieval of sales. Other than Sales, the API can also be used in interacting with other modules on Salesdock including Leads, Forms, Relations etc.

This documentation aims to provide comprehensive information on how developers can use the Salesdock API to build CRUD operations to manipulate information on Salesdock.

---

# API Basics

Each API resource that you use will have a clearly defined path structure. This documentation contains a reference for each resource in the API.

Before you can use these resources, below is the basic structure of an API resource:

## API Path

The Salesdock API path has the following structure:

`https://app.salesdock.nl/api/testomgeving/v1/user/{{resource_path}}`

\* [https://app.salesdock.nl](https://app.salesdock.nl) - The production URL is [https://app.salesdock.nl](https://app.salesdock.nl) and for development purpose you can use the staging URL at [https://app-staging.salesdock.nl](https://app-staging.salesdock.nl).  
\* testomgeving - This is your account domain name.  
\* v1 - Version number of the API. Current version is 'v1'.  
\* user - The scope can either be 'user' or 'account'. This is explained in more detail in the "Scope" section below. For some specific resources, the scope is not needed and this is mentioned in the documentation in corresponding places.  
\* {{resource_path}} - This indicates the path of the resource you want to access. Each resource path and its details are defined in this documentation under the 'RESOURCES' section. Examples of resource paths are: /products to retrieve all products or /products/3465 to retrieve details of a specific product.

## Requests

There are different approaches for making requests to our API. The command line tool cURL is easy and fast for testing our API. When you want to integrate the API into your own software, you can choose to use a general purpose REST library based on your application language. The documentation of each resource shows specific ways in which you can make request for each resource.

A REST API uses HTTP methods to determine which action to perform on the resource. GET, POST, PUT and DELETE are some of the common methods used in the Salesdock API resources. The type of method is also indicated in the resource documentation.

An eg. cURL call to get list of products would be:

`curl --location --request GET 'https://app.salesdock.nl/api/demo/v1/user/products' --header 'Authorization: Bearer ****' --header 'Accept: application/json'`

## Response

Each API request always returns an HTTP code as response. By looking at the code you can know the outcome of the API. Following response codes are returned by the Salesdock API:

| Code | Reason | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | Request was successful |
| 201 | Created | Record was created |
| 400 | Bad request | Request contains errors |
| 401 | Unauthorized | Wrong API token |
| 403 | Forbidden | No access to account/API token not whitelisted |
| 404 | Not found | Entity not found |
| 405 | Method Not Allowed | This endpoint is not available in this API |
| 429 / 1015 | Too many requests | Throttling limit reached (see next section) |
| 500 | Internal Error | Salesdock needs to fix this |

The structure of the API response generally follows the following template:

``` json
{
 "success": boolean, // Could be true or false, depending on if the API call was successful or not
  "data": array // Response data
  "message": "" // Success or failure messages
}

 ```

**Pagination**

Some API resources, return the data paginated. This is true for resources that might have a lot of information to return. The number of records per page is by default 20, although this can be configured in the Salesdock application. A sample structure of a paginated response is as follows:

``` json
{
 "success": boolean, // Could be true or false, depending on if the API call was successful or not
  "data": {
    "current_page": int, // Current page number
    "data": array, // This will contain all the returned data
    "first_page_url": string, // URL to the first page"
    "from": int, // First record in this page
    "last_page": int, // Last page number
    "last_page_url": URL to the last page,
    "next_page_url": null|int, URL to next page
    "path": string, // Resource path
    "per_page": int, Records shown per page
    "prev_page_url": null,
    "to": int, // Last record in this page
    "total": int // Number of total records
  }
  "message": "" // Success or failure messages
}

 ```

Some endpoints, support cursor pagination. This helps in retrieving results faster but will not show the total number of results in the response. The sample response of the respective endpoints will show if it supports cursor pagination or not. There is a difference in the response structure as shown below. In this case, the developers need to use next_page_url and prev_page_url to traverse between next and previous pages.

``` json
{
    "success": true,
    "data": {
        "path": "", // API URL path
        "per_page": int, // number of results per page
        "next_cursor": null|string, // token for next page
        "next_page_url": null|string, // API url for next page
        "prev_cursor": null|string, // token for previous page
        "prev_page_url": null|string, // API url for previous page
        "data": array // Response data
    },
    "message": "" // Response message
}

 ```

## Throttling (Rate limiting)

To prevent abuse of the Salesdock API, requests are throttled so that there is a limit to the number of requests that you can do. You can request the API at most 120 times every minute.

The throttling is based on the source IP and user. So only 120 requests can be requested per minute from one IP or by one API user.

The API will respond with a 429 or 1015; if you try to exceed this limit.

---

# Authentication

In order to access the Salesdock API, you have to be an authenticated user. Salesdock uses HTTP authentication via Bearer (token) authorization.

## Generating an API token

Your account admin can generate and provide you with the API token that you can use. Each API token is linked to a Salesdock user. Sometimes API tokens are IP specific and can only be accessed from the IP address(es) that were specified while creating the API token.

## Authorization

Once you have an API token, it can be passed in the header of the request to authenticate your request:

'Authorization: Bearer _\*\\_'

## Scope

There are 2 scopes for the API with different access privileges:

\* {{account}} - Account scope gives you access to information across the account.  
\* {{user}} - User scope gives you access for information that is only specific to the user.

Depending on the role of the user in the Salesdock application, you can use the above scopes in the API calls.

---

# Changelog

This section contains details about historical changes to the Salesdock API.

## 22-05-2026

- Added customer_classification support in Create Energy NL business sales
    

## 28-05-2025

- Endpoints to fetch statuses list for leads and forms
    

## 08-05-2025

- New endpoint to fetch Lead results by period
    

## 02-05-2025

- New endpoint to fetch Lead results by lead id
    

## 27-02-2025

- Team information added to Get sale endpoint
    

## 29-10-2024

- Multi-products sections information added to Get flow endpoint for default flows
    

## 14-10-2024

- Update lead endpoints accepts relation_id
    

## 10-10-2024

- Period search parameters added for Get products endpoint
    

## 26-09-2024

- Leads history added to Get leads and Get lead enpoints
    

## 12-07-2024

- Get sale endpoint changes to include products and totals
    

## 04-07-2024

- Get agreement endpoint
    

## 14-06-2024

- Changes in Get tasks and Get task endpoint to provide more information
    
- Added labels to Create Lead APIs
    

## 31-05-2024

- New endpoint to fetch townships
    
- Changes to create lead form endpoint
    

## 22-04-2024

- Extra fields and lead questions added to Get leads API response
    

## 09-04-2024

- Update sale proposal for telecom sales
    
- Update finalized sales for default and telecom sales
    

## 29-03-2024

- New endpoint to create optin for a sale
    

## 04-12-2023

- Update sale proposal end pooint for default sales
    

## 24-10-2023

- Product question rules as JSON
    

## 05-10-2023

- New API endpoints to update lead as reseller and admin users
    

## 18-09-2023

- New API endpoint to create, update and delete task
    

## 17-07-2023

- New API endpoint to retrieve products of an organisation.
    

## 20-06-2023

- New API endpoint to fetch lead forms
    
- New API endpoint to fetch lead form elements
    

## 02-06-2023

- New API endpoint to fetch lead activities
    
- New API endpoint to fetch lead sources
    

## 25-04-2023

- New API endpoint to update relation
    

## 13-03-2023

- Get sale API response updated to include more information
    

## 27-02-2023

- New API endpoint to update status of lead
    
- Changes in get product calculation endpoint to support multiple EAN codes.
    

## 13-01-2023

\* New API endpoint to fetch sale ids of form instance

## 05-12-2022

\* New API endpoint to fetch tasks

\* New API endpoints to get task

## 21-10-2022

\* New API endpoint to fetch Lead form PDF

## 13-10-2022

\* New API endpoint to get form instances based on Sale ID

## 20-08-2022

\* New API endpoint to retrieve products of a sale

## 26-08-2022

\* New API endpoint to retrieve product questions answers of a sale  
\* Added sale extra fields to the get sale API endpoint

## 09-08-2022

\* New API endpoint to create solar panel sale

## 06-06-2022

\* New API endpoint to connect organisations to product  
\* New API endpoint to connect product to oraganisations

## 03-06-2022

\* New API endpoints to create, update and delete organisations

## 02-06-2022

\* New API endpoints to create, update and delete products

## 19-05-2022

\* New API endpoints to view, create, update and delete suppliers

## 13-05-2022

\* Changes in create concept transaction to support source_version and source_metadata  
\* New endpoint to get sales of concept transaction  
\* New endpoint to get view URL of sale

## 10-02-2022

\* New API endpoint to create relation

## 17-01-2022

\* New API endpoint to fetch list of agreements

## 12-01-2022

\* New API endpoint to fetch list of leads

## 21-12-2021

\* New API endpoint to fetch Belgium energy products with tariff and cost specifications.

## 20-12-2021

\* New API endpoint to update energy product

## 03-11-2021

\* New API endpoints for fetching and creating form instances  
\* New API endpoint for fetching list of relations.

## 30-07-2021

\* New API endpoints added to get sales contracts

## 07-05-2021

\* New endpoints to create leads and fetch leads

## 16-04-2021

\* End point to get organisations and users

## 26-03-2021

\* New endpoints for commissions added

## 27-01-2021

\* API throttling changed to 60 calls per minute

## 25-01-2021

\* external_reference added to create sale end-points

## 17-11-2020

\* New API endpoint to update the status of a sale.

\* New API endpoint to get list of sale statuses.

## 21-09-2020

\* New API endpoint to get energy usage estimate.  
\* New API endpoint to get products with energy tariffs and calculations.

## 17-08-2020

\* User and organisation details added to create sale end points.  
\* New end point to get sales by different export templates.

## 24-07-2020

\* New endpoint added for getting energy sales.

## 25-06-2020

\* Parameters added in get sales call.  
\* Details on how to create completed/uncompleted offers and orders explained.

## 09-04-2020

\* Get sales response changed.

## 26-03-2020

\* Create concept transactions and lead APIs added.

## 19-02-2020

\* Agreements validation in Create sales API.

## 06-02-2020

\* First version of the API made public.

---

# Resources

The supported resources in the Salesdock API along with sample requests and responses can be seen in this section.

request.description:
Create an offer for energy flow (small-business). Replace {{flow_identifier}} with the identifier of the flow. Replace {{flow_type_identifier}} with the identifier of the flow type. In the questionData object, the product question identifiers needed for the flow should be sent along with the corresponding answers In the agreements object, the agreement identifiers needed for the flow should be sent along with value "1" for accepted agreements. #### Completed and uncompleted offers The API endpoint can be used to create both completed and uncompleted offers. Uncompleted offers result in a new contract pdf being generated and an email being sent to the customer to accept the offer. A completed offer will not send an offer acceptance email. An offer via the API is considered as a completed offer if it contains the "contract_date" parameter. **Body Parameters** | Field | Type | Mandatory | Possible Value(s) | Description | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | transaction_type | string | Y | offer | | | connection_postcode | string | Y | | Connection postcode | | connection_housenumber | integer | Y | | Connection house number | | connection_suffix | string | N | | Connection house number extension | | connection_streetname | string | Y | | Connection street name | | connection_city | string | Y | | Connection city | | postcode | string | Y | | Correspondence postcode | | housenumber | string | Y | | Correspondence house number | | suffix | string | Y | | Correspondence house number extension | | streetname | string | Y | | Correspondence street name | | city | string | Y | | Correspondence city | | gender | string | N | | | | firstname | string | Y | | | | lastname | string | Y | | | | email | string | Y | | | | phone | string | Y | | | | contact_person | string | Y | | Contact person | | company_name | string | Y | | Company name | | company_coc | string | Y | | KVK Number | | company_vat | string | N | | BTW number of Company. Required according to flow settings | | iban | string | N | | Required according to flow settings | | iban_holder | string | N | | Required according to flow settings | | sale_channel | string | N | 'd2d', 'retail', 'telemarketing', 'online', 'f2f' | | | product_id | integer | Y | | ID of product | | business | string | Y | 1 | | | type | string | Y | 'e', 'g', 'e_g' | Type of energy selected - Electricity, Gas or electricity and gas | | building_function | integer | Y | 0, 1 | Woon-/verblijfsfunctie | | tarifftype | integer | Y | 0, 1 | 0 => single meter; 1 => double meter | | usage_e_single | float | N | | Required if type is 'e' or 'e_g' and tarifftype is 0, Single meter usage of electricity | | usage_e_high | float | N | | Required if type is 'e' or 'e_g' and tarifftype is 1, Normaal usage of electricity in the case of double meter | | usage_e_low | float | N | | Required if type is 'e' or 'e_g' and tarifftype is 1, Low usage of electricity in the case of double meter | | usage_g | string | N | | Required if type is 'g' or 'e_g', Gas usage | | has_return | integer | Y | 0, 1 | Ik lever ook elektriciteit terug, 0 => No; 1 => Yes | | return | float | N | | Required if has_return is 1 and tarifftype is 0, Single meter return of electricity | | return_e_high | float | N | | Required if has_return is 1 and tarifftype is 1, Double meter return of normaal electricity | | return_e_low | float | N | | Required if has_return is 1 and tarifftype is 1, Double meter return of low electricity | | e_gridoperator | string | N | | Electricity grid operator is required if type is 'e' or 'e_g'. If empty, then grid operator is obtained from eancodeboek.nl. | | g_gridoperator | string | N | | Gas grid operator is required if type is 'g' or 'e_g'. If empty, then grid operator is obtained from eancodeboek.nl. | | e_connection_type | string | N | 'E_NULTARIEF', 'E_1X6A_ONB' , ‘E_1X6A', 'E_1X10A_ONB', 'E_1X10A', 'E_3X25A_ONB', 'E_3X25A', ‘E_3X35A', 'E_3X50A', 'E_3X63A', 'E_3X80A',
'E_GV' | Electricity connection type is required if type is 'e' or 'e_g'. If empty, then value is estimated based on the usage. | | g_connection_type | string | N | 'G_NULTARIEF', 'G_ONB', 'G_G6_500’, ‘G_G6_500_4000', 'G_G6_4000', 'G_G10', 'G_G16', 'G_G25',
'G_GV' | Gas connection type is required if type is 'g' or 'e_g'. If empty, then value is estimated based on the usage. | | g_region | integer | N | \[1-10\] | Gas region is required if type is 'g' or 'e_g'. If empty, value is obtained from database. | | electricity_ean | string | N | | Electricity EAN Code is required if type is 'e' or 'e_g'. If empty, then ean code is obtained from eancodeboek.nl. | | gas_ean | string | N | | Gas EAN Code is required if type is 'g' or 'e_g'. If empty, then ean code is obtained from eancodeboek.nl. | | contract_date | date | N | | In DD-MM-YYYY format. If not empty, then signifies that it is a completed offer. | | contract_pdf | array | N | | Required if contract_date is not empty. Array expects 3 keys. First one is "content", which is the Base64 string of the contract pdf. Second one is "extension", which should always be 'pdf'. Third one is "created_at", which is the file created date time in "YYYY-mm-dd hh:mm:ss" format | | signature | string | N | | Required based on flow settings, Base64 encoded string of image | | questionData | array | Y | | Product question identifiers along with corresponding answers | | agreements | array | Y | | Agreement identifiers with answer as "1" for accepted agreements | | initial_user_firstname | string | N | | The first name of the user who did the sale | | initial_user_lastname | string | N | | The last name of the user who did the sale | | initial_organisation_name | string | N | | The name of the organisation which did the sale | | initial_created_at | datetime | N | | In YYYY-mm-dd hh:mm:ss format. The date and time on which the sale was originally created | | external_reference | string | N | | An order reference identifier that can be used by the API partners | | relation_id | integer | N | | The ID of the relation that needs to be linked to the sale. This has to be a valid relation ID that is accessible to the user. If relation_id is not sent, then a new relation will be created and linked to the sale. | | customer_classification | string | N | "micro_business", "small_business, "medium_business", "large_business" | |

request.description:
Create an order for energy flow (small-business).  
Replace {{flow_dentifier}} with the identifier of the flow.

Replace {{flow_type_identifier}} with the identifier of the flow type.

In the questionData object, the product question identifiers needed for the flow should be sent along with the corresponding answers

In the agreements object, the agreement identifiers needed for the flow should be sent along with value "1" for accepted agreements.

#### Completed and uncompleted orders

The API endpoint can be used to create both completed and uncompleted orders. Uncompleted orders result in a new contract pdf being generated and also a confirmation/verification email being sent to customers based on flow settings. A completed order will not send an email. An order via the API is considered as a completed order if it contains the "contract_date" parameter.

**Body Parameters**

| Field | Type | Mandatory | Possible Value(s) | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| transaction_type | string | Y | order |  |
| connection_postcode | string | Y |  | Connection postcode |
| connection_housenumber | integer | Y |  | Connection house number |
| connection_suffix | string | N |  | Connection house number extension |
| connection_streetname | string | Y |  | Connection street name |
| connection_city | string | Y |  | Connection city |
| postcode | string | Y |  | Correspondence postcode |
| housenumber | string | Y |  | Correspondence house number |
| suffix | string | Y |  | Correspondence house number extension |
| streetname | string | Y |  | Correspondence street name |
| city | string | Y |  | Correspondence city |
| gender | string | N |  |  |
| firstname | string | Y |  |  |
| lastname | string | Y |  |  |
| email | string | Y |  |  |
| phone | string | Y |  |  |
| contact_person | string | Y |  | Contact person |
| company_name | string | Y |  | Company name |
| company_coc | string | Y |  | KVK Number |
| company_vat | string | N |  | Required according to flow settings |
| iban | string | N |  | Required according to flow settings |
| iban_holder | string | N |  | Required according to flow settings |
| sale_channel | string | N | 'd2d', 'retail', 'telemarketing', 'online', 'f2f' |  |
| product_id | integer | Y |  | ID of product |
| business | string | Y | 1 |  |
| type | string | Y | 'e', 'g', 'e_g' | Type of energy selected - Electricity, Gas or electricity and gas |
| building_function | integer | Y | 0, 1 | Woon-/verblijfsfunctie |
| tarifftype | integer | Y | 0, 1 | 0 => single meter; 1 => double meter |
| usage_e_single | float | N |  | Required if type is 'e' or 'e_g' and tarifftype is 0, Single meter usage of electricity |
| usage_e_high | float | N |  | Required if type is 'e' or 'e_g' and tarifftype is 1, Normaal usage of electricity in the case of double meter |
| usage_e_low | float | N |  | Required if type is 'e' or 'e_g' and tarifftype is 1, Low usage of electricity in the case of double meter |
| usage_g | string | N |  | Required if type is 'g' or 'e_g', Gas usage |
| has_return | integer | Y | 0, 1 | Ik lever ook elektriciteit terug, 0 => No; 1 => Yes |
| return | float | N |  | Required if has_return is 1 and tarifftype is 0, Single meter return of electricity |
| return_e_high | float | N |  | Required if has_return is 1 and tarifftype is 1, Double meter return of normaal electricity |
| return_e_low | float | N |  | Required if has_return is 1 and tarifftype is 1, Double meter return of low electricity |
| e_gridoperator | string | N |  | Electricity grid operator is required if type is 'e' or 'e_g'. If empty, then grid operator is obtained from eancodeboek.nl. |
| g_gridoperator | string | N |  | Gas grid operator is required if type is 'g' or 'e_g'. If empty, then grid operator is obtained from eancodeboek.nl. |
| e_connection_type | string | N | 'E_NULTARIEF', 'E_1X6A_ONB' , ‘E_1X6A', 'E_1X10A_ONB', 'E_1X10A', 'E_3X25A_ONB', 'E_3X25A', ‘E_3X35A', 'E_3X50A', 'E_3X63A', 'E_3X80A', 'E_GV' | Electricity connection type is required if type is 'e' or 'e_g'. If empty, then value is estimated based on the usage. |
| g_connection_type | string | N | 'G_NULTARIEF', 'G_ONB', 'G_G6_500’, ‘G_G6_500_4000', 'G_G6_4000', 'G_G10', 'G_G16', 'G_G25', 'G_GV' | Gas connection type is required if type is 'g' or 'e_g'. If empty, then value is estimated based on the usage. |
| g_region | integer | N | \[1-10\] | Gas region is required if type is 'g' or 'e_g'. If empty, value is obtained from database. |
| electricity_ean | string | N |  | Electricity EAN Code is required if type is 'e' or 'e_g'. If empty, then ean code is obtained from eancodeboek.nl. |
| gas_ean | string | N |  | Gas EAN Code is required if type is 'g' or 'e_g'. If empty, then ean code is obtained from eancodeboek.nl. |
| contract_date | date | N |  | In DD-MM-YYYY format. If not empty, then signifies that it is a completed order. |
| contract_pdf | array | N |  | Required if contract_date is not empty. Array expects 3 keys. First one is "content", which is the Base64 string of the contract pdf. Second one is "extension", which should always be 'pdf'. Third one is "created_at", which is the file created date time in "YYYY-mm-dd hh:mm:ss" format |
| signature | string | N |  | Required based on flow settings, Base64 encoded string of image |
| questionData | array | Y |  | Product question identifiers along with corresponding answers |
| agreements | array | Y |  | Agreement identifiers with answer as "1" for accepted agreements |
| initial_user_firstname | string | N |  | The first name of the user who did the sale |
| initial_user_lastname | string | N |  | The last name of the user who did the sale |
| initial_organisation_name | string | N |  | The name of the organisation which did the sale |
| initial_created_at | datetime | N |  | In YYYY-mm-dd hh:mm:ss format. The date and time on which the sale was originally created. If the sale is completed, then this date time (if not empty), is considered as the contract signed date. |
| external_reference | string | N |  | An order reference identifier that can be used by the API partners |
| relation_id | integer | N |  | The ID of the relation that needs to be linked to the sale. This has to be a valid relation ID that is accessible to the user. If relation_id is not sent, then a new relation will be created and linked to the sale. |
| customer_classification | string | N | "micro_business", "small_business, "medium_business", "large_business" |  |

request.description:
Create an offer for energy flow (consumer).  
Replace {{flow_dentifier}} with the identifier of the flow.

Replace {{flow_type_identifier}} with the identifier of the flow type.

In the questionData object, the product question identifiers needed for the flow should be sent along with the corresponding answers

In the agreements object, the agreement identifiers needed for the flow should be sent along with value "1" for accepted agreements.

#### Completed and uncompleted offers

The API endpoint can be used to create both completed and uncompleted offers. Uncompleted offers result in a new contract pdf being generated and an email being sent to the customer to accept the offer. A completed offer will not send an offer acceptance email. An offer via the API is considered as a completed offer if it contains the "contract_date" parameter.

**Body Parameters**

| Field | Type | Mandatory | Possible Value(s) | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| transaction_type | string | Y | offer |  |
| connection_postcode | string | Y |  | Connection postcode |
| connection_housenumber | integer | Y |  | Connection house number |

| product_id | integer | Y |  | ID of product |
| business | string | Y | 0 |  |
| type | string | Y | 'e', 'g', 'e_g' | Type of energy selected - Electricity, Gas or electricity and gas |

| usage_g | string | N |  | Required if type is 'g' or 'e_g', Gas usage |
| has_return | integer | Y | 0, 1 | Ik lever ook elektriciteit terug, 0 => No; 1 => Yes |
| return | float | N |  | Required if has_return is 1 and tarifftype is 0, Single meter return of electricity |
| return_e_high | float | N |  | Required if has_return is 1 and tarifftype is 1, Double meter return of normaal electricity |
| return_e_low | float | N |  | Required if has_return is 1 and tarifftype is 1, Double meter return of low electricity |
| e_gridoperator | string | N |  | Electricity grid operator is required if type is 'e' or 'e_g'. If empty, then grid operator is obtained from eancodeboek.nl. |
| g_gridoperator | string | N |  | Gas grid operator is required if type is 'g' or 'e_g'. If empty, then grid operator is obtained from eancodeboek.nl. |
| e_connection_type | string | N | 'E_NULTARIEF', 'E_1X6A_ONB' , ‘E_1X6A', 'E_1X10A_ONB', 'E_1X10A', 'E_3X25A_ONB', 'E_3X25A', ‘E_3X35A', 'E_3X50A', 'E_3X63A', 'E_3X80A', 'E_GV' | Electricity connection type is required if type is 'e' or 'e_g'. If empty, then value is estimated based on the usage. |
| g_connection_type | string | N | 'G_NULTARIEF', 'G_ONB', 'G_G6_500’, ‘G_G6_500_4000', 'G_G6_4000', 'G_G10', 'G_G16', 'G_G25', 'G_GV' | Gas connection type is required if type is 'g' or 'e_g'. If empty, then value is estimated based on the usage. |

| gas_ean | string | N |  | Gas EAN Code is required if type is 'g' or 'e_g'. If empty, then ean code is obtained from eancodeboek.nl. |

Second one is "extension", which should always be 'pdf'. Third one is "created_at", which is the file created date time in "YYYY-mm-dd hh:mm:ss" format |
| signature | string | N |  | Required based on flow settings, Base64 encoded string of image |

request.description:
Create an order for energy flow (consumer).  
Replace {{flow_dentifier}} with the identifier of the flow.

Replace {{flow_type_identifier}} with the identifier of the flow type.

In the questionData object, get the product question identifiers needed for the flow and send them along with corresponding answers.

In the agreements object, get the agreement identifiers needed for the flow and send them along with value "1" for accepted agreements.

#### Completed and uncompleted orders

The API endpoint can be used to create both completed and uncompleted orders. Uncompleted orders result in a new contract pdf being generated and also a confirmation/verification email being sent to customers based on flow settings. A completed order will not send an email. An order via the API is considered as a completed order if it contains the "contract_date" parameter.

**Body Parameters**

| Field | Type | Mandatory | Possible Value(s) | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| transaction_type | string | Y | order |  |
| connection_postcode | string | Y |  | Connection postcode |
| connection_housenumber | integer | Y |  | Connection house number |
| connection_suffix | string | N |  | Connection house number extension |
| connection_streetname | string | Y |  | Connection street name |
| connection_city | string | Y |  | Connection city |
| postcode | string | Y |  | Contractant postcode |
| housenumber | string | Y |  | Contractant house number |
| suffix | string | Y |  | Contractant house number extension |
| streetname | string | Y |  | Contractant street name |
| city | string | Y |  | Contractant city |
| gender | string | N |  |  |
| firstname | string | Y |  |  |
| lastname | string | Y |  |  |
| birthdate | string | Y |  | DOB in format dd-mm-yyyy |
| email | string | Y |  |  |
| phone | string | Y |  |  |
| iban | string | N |  | Required according to flow settings |
| iban_holder | string | N |  | Required according to flow settings |
| sale_channel | string | N | 'd2d', 'retail', 'telemarketing', 'online', 'f2f' |  |
| product_id | integer | Y |  | ID of product |
| business | string | Y | 0 |  |
| type | string | Y | 'e', 'g', 'e_g' | Type of energy selected - Electricity, Gas or electricity and gas |
| building_function | integer | Y | 0, 1 | Woon-/verblijfsfunctie |
| tarifftype | integer | Y | 0, 1 | 0 => single meter; 1 => double meter |
| usage_e_single | float | N |  | Required if type is 'e' or 'e_g' and tarifftype is 0, Single meter usage of electricity |
| usage_e_high | float | N |  | Required if type is 'e' or 'e_g' and tarifftype is 1, Normaal usage of electricity in the case of double meter |
| usage_e_low | float | N |  | Required if type is 'e' or 'e_g' and tarifftype is 1, Low usage of electricity in the case of double meter |
| usage_g | string | N |  | Required if type is 'g' or 'e_g', Gas usage |
| has_return | integer | Y | 0, 1 | Ik lever ook elektriciteit terug, 0 => No; 1 => Yes |
| return | float | N |  | Required if has_return is 1 and tarifftype is 0, Single meter return of electricity |
| return_e_high | float | N |  | Required if has_return is 1 and tarifftype is 1, Double meter return of normaal electricity |
| return_e_low | float | N |  | Required if has_return is 1 and tarifftype is 1, Double meter return of low electricity |
| e_gridoperator | string | N |  | Electricity grid operator is required if type is 'e' or 'e_g'. If empty, then grid operator is obtained from eancodeboek.nl. |
| g_gridoperator | string | N |  | Gas grid operator is required if type is 'g' or 'e_g'. If empty, then grid operator is obtained from eancodeboek.nl. |
| e_connection_type | string | N | 'E_NULTARIEF', 'E_1X6A_ONB' , ‘E_1X6A', 'E_1X10A_ONB', 'E_1X10A', 'E_3X25A_ONB', 'E_3X25A', ‘E_3X35A', 'E_3X50A', 'E_3X63A', 'E_3X80A', 'E_GV | Electricity connection type is required if type is 'e' or 'e_g'. If empty, then value is estimated based on the usage. |
| g_connection_type | string | N | 'G_NULTARIEF', 'G_ONB', 'G_G6_500’, ‘G_G6_500_4000', 'G_G6_4000', 'G_G10', 'G_G16', 'G_G25', 'G_GV' | Gas connection type is required if type is 'g' or 'e_g'. If empty, then value is estimated based on the usage. |
| g_region | integer | N | \[1-10\] | Gas region is required if type is 'g' or 'e_g'. If empty, value is obtained from database. |
| electricity_ean | string | N |  | Electricity EAN Code is required if type is 'e' or 'e_g'. If empty, then ean code is obtained from eancodeboek.nl. |
| gas_ean | string | N |  | Gas EAN Code is required if type is 'g' or 'e_g'. If empty, then ean code is obtained from eancodeboek.nl. |
| contract_date | date | N |  | In DD-MM-YYYY format. If not empty, then signifies that it is a completed order. |
| contract_pdf | array | N |  | Required if contract_date is not empty. Array expects 3 keys. First one is "content", which is the Base64 string of the contract pdf. Second one is "extension", which should always be 'pdf'. Third one is "created_at", which is the file created date time in "YYYY-mm-dd hh:mm:ss" format |
| signature | string | N |  | Required based on flow settings, Base64 encoded string of image |
| questionData | array | Y |  | Product question identifiers along with corresponding answers |
| agreements | array | Y |  | Agreement identifiers with answer as "1" for accepted agreements |
| initial_user_firstname | string | N |  | The first name of the user who did the sale |
| initial_user_lastname | string | N |  | The last name of the user who did the sale |
| initial_organisation_name | string | N |  | The name of the organisation which did the sale |
| initial_created_at | datetime | N |  | In YYYY-mm-dd hh:mm:ss format. The date and time on which the sale was originally created. If the sale is completed, then this date time (if not empty), is considered as the contract signed date. |
| external_reference | string | N |  | An order reference identifier that can be used by the API partners |
| relation_id | integer | N |  | The ID of the relation that needs to be linked to the sale. This has to be a valid relation ID that is accessible to the user. If relation_id is not sent, then a new relation will be created and linked to the sale. |

request.description:
This endpoint helps in updating the tariffs and extra field values of an energie product.

Replace the product_id in the URL with the id of the product.

The identifiers to use for tariffs and extra fields can be received from the "Get product" API call

By default the tariffs are mandatory in the request. But if only the extra fields need to be updated and not the tariffs, send do_not_update_tariffs = true, in the request body.

**Body Parameters**

| **Field** | **Format** | **Mandatory** | **Possible Value(s)** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| type | string | Y | 'energy_e_g', 'energy_e', 'energy_g' | This is type of the prodocut. energy_e_g - Electricity and gas energy_e - Only electricity energy_g - Only gas |
| active | string | Y | '1', '0' | If product is active or inactive. 1 = active and 0 = inactive |
| valid_from | date (dd-mm-yyyy) | N |  | Starting date from which product can be sold. Do not send if no start date required |
| valid_till | date (dd-mm-yyyy) | N |  | Ending date till which product can be sold. Do not send if no end date required |
| product_type |  | Y | 'fixed', 'variable', 'dynamic' | The type of energie product |
| tariff_single | numeric | N |  | Single meter electricity tariff |
| tariff_high | numeric | N |  | Peak electricity tariff |
| tariff_low | numeric | N |  | Off peak electricity tariff |
| tariff_high_t2 | numeric | N |  |  |
| tariff_low_t2 | numeric | N |  |  |
| tariff_return | numeric | N |  | Electricity return tariff single meter |
| tariff_return_high | numeric | N |  | Electricity peak return tariff |
| tariff_return_low | numeric | N |  | Electricity off peak return tariff |
| tariff_fixed_e | numeric | N |  | Fixed electricity tariff |
| g_region_tax_level | string | N | supplier, product | If the supplier gas tax level is on supplier level or product level. |
| tariff_g | numeric | N |  | Gas usage tariff |
| tariff_g_g2 | numeric | N |  | Gas usage tariff (G2) |
| tariff_fixed_g | numeric | N |  | Gas fixed tariff |

item.name: Get energy product with tariff and calculation

query.description: Postcode of the address
House number of the address
House number extension of the address (if no suffix, do not send this field)
e_g = Electricity and gas, e = only electricity, g = only gas
1= Double meter, 0 = single meter
Single usage electricity. Required if: type = e/e_g and tarifftype = 0.
High usage electricity. Required if: type = e/e_g and tarifftype = 1.
Low usage electricity. Required if: type = e/e_g and tarifftype = 1.
Usage gasRequired if: type = g/e_g.
1 = has electricity return, 0 = no electricity return. Required if: type = e/e_g.
Single meter electricity return. Required if has_return = 1 and tarifftype = 0.
Electricity return high. Required if has_return = 1 and tarifftype = 1.
Elextricity return low. Required if has_return = 1 and tarifftype = 1.
1 = yes, 0 = no
Electricity connection type. Possible values: 'E_NULTARIEF', 'E_1X6A_ONB' , ‘E_1X6A', 'E_1X10A_ONB', 'E_1X10A', 'E_3X25A_ONB', 'E_3X25A', ‘E_3X35A', 'E_3X50A', 'E_3X63A', 'E_3X80A', 'E_GV'
Gas connection type. Possible values: 'G_NULTARIEF', 'G_ONB', 'G_G6_500’, ‘G_G6_500_4000', 'G_G6_4000', 'G_G10', 'G_G16', 'G_G25', 'G_GV'
Identifier of the flow

request.description: This end point returns a product specified in the product id along with the energy tariffs and calculations

response.name: Get energy product with tariff and calculation

header.value: script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://cdn.jsdelivr.net https://js-agent.newrelic.com https://bam.nr-data.net https://bam.eu01.nr-data.net https://maps.googleapis.com; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://fonts.googleapis.com; img-src 'self' data: https:; font-src 'self' data: https: https://fonts.gstatic.com; connect-src 'self'; form-action 'self'; frame-ancestors 'self'; frame-src 'self'; object-src 'self'

accelerometer 'self'; ambient-light-sensor 'self'; autoplay 'self'; battery 'self'; camera 'self'; display-capture 'self'; document-domain *; encrypted-media 'self'; execution-while-not-rendered *; execution-while-out-of-viewport *; fullscreen 'self'; geolocation 'self'; gyroscope 'self'; layout-animations 'self'; legacy-image-formats 'self'; magnetometer 'self'; microphone 'self'; midi 'self'; navigation-override 'self'; oversized-images *; payment 'self'; picture-in-picture *; publickey-credentials 'self'; sync-xhr *; unoptimized-images 'self'; unsized-media *; usb 'self'; wake-lock 'self'; xr-spatial-tracking 'self'

request.description:
Create an order/offer for Belgium energy flow. Replace {{flow_dentifier}} with the identifier of the flow. Replace {{flow_type_identifier}} with the identifier of the flow type. In the questionData object, get the product question identifiers needed for the flow and send them along with corresponding answers. In the agreements object, get the agreement identifiers needed for the flow and send them along with value "1" for accepted agreements. #### Completed and uncompleted orders The API endpoint can be used to create both completed and uncompleted orders. Uncompleted orders result in a new contract pdf being generated and also a confirmation/verification email being sent to customers based on flow settings. A completed order will not send an email. An order via the API is considered as a completed order if it contains the "contract_date" parameter. **Body Parameters** | Field | Type | Mandatory | Possible Value(s) | Description | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | transaction_type | string | Y | order/offer | | | connection_postcode | string | Y | | Connection postcode | | connection_housenumber | integer | Y | | Connection house number | | connection_suffix | string | N | | Connection house number extension | | connection_streetname | string | Y | | Connection street name | | connection_city | string | Y | | Connection city | | postcode | string | Y | | Correspondence postcode | | housenumber | string | Y | | Correspondence house number | | suffix | string | Y | | Correspondence house number extension | | streetname | string | Y | | Correspondence street name | | city | string | Y | | Correspondence city | | gender | string | N | | | | firstname | string | Y | | | | lastname | string | Y | | | | birthdate | string | Y | | DOB in format dd-mm-yyyy | | email | string | Y | | | | phone | string | Y | | | | iban | string | N | | Required according to flow settings | | iban_holder | string | N | | Required according to flow settings | | sale_channel | string | N | 'd2d', 'retail', 'telemarketing', 'online', 'f2f' | | | product_id | integer | Y | | ID of product | | business | integer | Y | 1,0 | 1=business;0=consumer | | type | string | Y | 'e', 'g', 'e_g' | Type of energy selected - Electricity, Gas or electricity and gas | | e_measurement_frequency | string | Y | 'YMR', 'MMR', 'AMR' | Electricity measurement frequency | | g_measurement_frequency | string | Y | 'YMR', 'MMR', 'AMR' | Gas measurement frequency | | tarifftype | integer | Y | 0, 1 | 0 => single meter; 1 => double meter | | usage_e_single | int | N | | Required if type is 'e' or 'e_g' and tarifftype is 0, Single meter usage of electricity | | usage_e_high | int | N | | Required if type is 'e' or 'e_g' and tarifftype is 1, Normaal usage of electricity in the case of double meter | | usage_e_low | int | N | | Required if type is 'e' or 'e_g' and tarifftype is 1, Low usage of electricity in the case of double meter | | usage_g | int | N | | Required if type is 'g' or 'e_g', Gas usage | | exclusive_night | string | Y | 1,0 | 1=Exclusive night meter; 0 = Not exclusive | | usage_e_exclusive | string | N | | Electricity usage if exclusive_night = 1 | | has_return | integer | Y | 0, 1 | Ik lever ook elektriciteit terug, 0 => No; 1 => Yes | | return | int | N | | Required if has_return is 1 and tarifftype is 0, Single meter return of electricity | | return_e_high | int | N | | Required if has_return is 1 and tarifftype is 1, Double meter return of normaal electricity | | return_e_low | int | N | | Required if has_return is 1 and tarifftype is 1, Double meter return of low electricity | | connection_region_code | string | Y | 'brussels', 'flanders', 'wallonia' | The region of the address | | has_digital_meter | string | N | 0, 1 | 0 => Digital meter not present; 1 => Digital meter is present
By default 0 is taken | | converter_power | float | N | Between 0 and 10 | Required for wallonia when has_digital_meter = 1 or 0. Required for flanders when has_digital_meter = 0 | | average_monthly_peak | float | N | Greater than or equal to 2.5 | | | e_grid_operator | string | N | wallonia: ORES (NAMUR), ORES (HAINAUT), ORES (EST), ORES (LUXEMBOURG), ORES (VERVIERS), ORES (BRABANT WALLON), ORES (MOUSCRON), RESA, AIEG, AIESH, RÉSEAU DES ÉNERGIES DE WAVRE flanders: FLUVIUS HALLE-VILVOORDE, FLUVIUS ANTWERPEN, FLUVIUS LIMBURG, FLUVIUS WEST, FLUVIUS IMEWO, FLUVIUS MIDDEN-VLAANDEREN, FLUVIUS KEMPEN, FLUVIUS ZENNE-DIJLE | Electricity grid operator is required if type is 'e' or 'e_g'. Depends on the region | | g_grid_operator | string | N | wallonia: ORES (NAMUR), ORES (HAINAUT), ORES (EST), ORES (LUXEMBOURG), ORES (VERVIERS), ORES (BRABANT WALLON), ORES (MOUSCRON), RESA
flanders: FLUVIUS HALLE-VILVOORDE, FLUVIUS ANTWERPEN, FLUVIUS LIMBURG, FLUVIUS WEST, FLUVIUS IMEWO, FLUVIUS MIDDEN-VLAANDEREN, FLUVIUS KEMPEN, FLUVIUS ZENNE-DIJLE | Gas grid operator is required if type is 'g' or 'e_g'. Depends on the region | | contract_date | date | N | | In DD-MM-YYYY format. If not empty, then signifies that it is a completed order. | | contract_pdf | array | N | | Required if contract_date is not empty. Array expects 3 keys. First one is "content", which is the Base64 string of the contract pdf. Second one is "extension", which should always be 'pdf'. Third one is "created_at", which is the file created date time in "YYYY-mm-dd hh:mm:ss" format | | signature | string | N | | Required based on flow settings, Base64 encoded string of image | | questionData | array | Y | | Product question identifiers along with corresponding answers | | agreements | array | Y | | Agreement identifiers with answer as "1" for accepted agreements | | initial_user_firstname | string | N | | The first name of the user who did the sale | | initial_user_lastname | string | N | | The last name of the user who did the sale | | initial_organisation_name | string | N | | The name of the organisation which did the sale | | initial_created_at | datetime | N | | In YYYY-mm-dd hh:mm:ss format. The date and time on which the sale was originally created. If the sale is completed, then this date time (if not empty), is considered as the contract signed date. | | external_reference | string | N | | An order reference identifier that can be used by the API partners | | relation_id | integer | N | | The ID of the relation that needs to be linked to the sale. This has to be a valid relation ID that is accessible to the user. If relation_id is not sent, then a new relation will be created and linked to the sale. |

query.description: Postcode of the address
House number of the address
House number extension of the address (if no suffix, do not send this field)
e_g = Electricity and gas, e = only electricity, g = only gas
1= Double meter, 0 = single meter
Single usage electricity. Required if: type = e/e_g and tarifftype = 0.
High usage electricity. Required if: type = e/e_g and tarifftype = 1.
Low usage electricity. Required if: type = e/e_g and tarifftype = 1.
1 = has electricity return, 0 = no electricity return. Required if: type = e/e_g.
Single meter electricity return. Required if has_return = 1 and tarifftype = 0.
Electricity usage return high. Required if has_return = 1 and tarifftype = 1.
Electricity usage return low. Required if has_return = 1 and tarifftype = 1.
Gas usage. Required if: type = g/e_g.

Electricity: Electricity grid operator (optional). Based on region. Possible values: 
wallonia:
ORES (NAMUR), ORES (HAINAUT), ORES (EST), ORES (LUXEMBOURG), ORES (VERVIERS), ORES (BRABANT WALLON), ORES (MOUSCRON), RESA, AIEG, AIESH, RÉSEAU DES ÉNERGIES DE WAVRE
flanders:
FLUVIUS HALLE-VILVOORDE, FLUVIUS ANTWERPEN, FLUVIUS LIMBURG, FLUVIUS WEST, FLUVIUS IMEWO, FLUVIUS MIDDEN-VLAANDEREN, FLUVIUS KEMPEN, FLUVIUS ZENNE-DIJLE

Gas: Gas grid operator (optional). Based on region. Possible values: 
wallonia:
ORES (NAMUR), ORES (HAINAUT), ORES (EST), ORES (LUXEMBOURG), ORES (VERVIERS), ORES (BRABANT WALLON), ORES (MOUSCRON), RESA
flanders:
FLUVIUS HALLE-VILVOORDE, FLUVIUS ANTWERPEN, FLUVIUS LIMBURG, FLUVIUS WEST, FLUVIUS IMEWO, FLUVIUS MIDDEN-VLAANDEREN, FLUVIUS KEMPEN, FLUVIUS ZENNE-DIJLE
1= true, 0 = false
Exclusive night usage, If exlusive_night = 1;
Possible values - flanders, wallonia, brussels
Identifier of the flow
The coverter powe. Accepts number values greater than or equal to 10
If digital meter is available or not. Possible values are: 0,1
If charging station is available or not. Possible values are: 0,1
The average monthloy peak. Accepts numeric values greater than 2.5

request.description:
This endpoint fetches the product(s) along with the energy tariffs and calculations for Belgium.

By default it fetches all the products matching the parameters. To fetch the details of only one product, pass product_id in the request URL along with the id of the product

request.description:
Get all energy sales during a specific period using different filters. **Filter Parameters** | Parameter Name | Possible Value(s) or Type | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | period_filter_on | 'created_date', 'updated_date', 'finalized_at', 'cancelled_date', 'sent_date', 'deleted_date' | The date field to filter on.
created_date - Sale created date.
updated_date - Sale last updated date.
finalized_at - Sale signed date.
cancelled_date - Sale cancelled date.
sent_date - Sale processed date.
deleted_date - Sale deleted date.
| | period | 'today', 'yesterday', 'this_week', 'last_week', 'last_30_days', 'this_month', 'last_month', 'custom' | Possible periods to filter on | | period_start | Date in YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss format | If period is custom | | period_end | Date in YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss format | If period is custom | | type | 'sale', 'offer' | To filter direct sales or offers | | flow_id | integer | The id of the flow of the transaction | | supplier_id | integer | The id of the supplier of the product of the transaction | | statuses | array | The possible statuses can be retrieved using the "Get sale statuses list" endpoint Multiple statuses can be passed. Eg: statuses\[0\]=new&statuses\[1\]=sent | | offer_statuses | 'open', '1' (accepted), '0' (declined), '2' (expired) | The status of the offers of the transactions. Multiple offer statuses can be filtered. Eg. offer_statuses\[0\]=open&offer_statuses\[1\]=1 | | product_types | array | The type of the product of the transaction. Multiple product types can be passed like: product_types\[0\]=simple&product_types\[1\]=default | | sale_ids | array | The ids of the sales can be passed as an array to return specific sales like: sale_ids\[0\]=123456&sale_ids\[1\]=5555555 |

response.name: Get energy transactions

request.description:
Create offer for telecom flow.

Replace {{flow_dentifier}} with the identifier of the flow.

Replace {{flow_type_identifier}} with the identifier of the flow type.

In the questionData object, the product question identifiers needed for the flow should be sent along with the corresponding answers

In the agreements object, the agreement identifiers needed for the flow should be sent along with value "1" for accepted agreements.

In the related_products object, the related products that are chosen in the telecom flow need to be sent.

#### Completed and uncompleted offers

The API endpoint can be used to create both completed and uncompleted offers. Uncompleted offers result in a new contract pdf being generated and an email being sent to the customer to accept the offer. A completed offer will not send an offer acceptance email. An offer via the API is considered as a completed offer if it contains the "contract_date" parameter.

**Body Parameters**

| Field | Type | Mandatory | Possible Value(s) | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| transaction_type | string | Y | offer |  |
| connection_postcode | string | Y |  | Connection postcode |
| connection_housenumber | integer | Y |  | Connection house number |
| connection_suffix | string | N |  | Connection house number extension |
| connection_streetname | string | Y |  | Connection street name |
| connection_city | string | Y |  | Connection city |
| postcode | string | Y |  | Correspondence postcode |
| housenumber | string | Y |  | Correspondence house number |
| suffix | string | N |  | Correspondence house number extension |
| streetname | string | Y |  | Correspondence street name |
| city | string | Y |  | Correspondence city |
| gender | string | N |  |  |
| firstname | string | Y |  |  |
| lastname | string | Y |  |  |
| birthdate | string | Y |  | DOB in format dd-mm-yyyy |
| email | string | Y |  |  |
| phone | string | Y |  |  |
| iban | string | N |  | Required according to flow settings |
| iban_holder | string | N |  | Required according to flow settings |
| sale_channel | string | N | 'd2d', 'retail', 'telemarketing', 'online', 'f2f' |  |
| product_id | integer | Y |  | ID of product |
| business | integer | Y | 1, 0 | 1 = business, 0 = consumer |
| contact_person | string | Y, if business = 1 |  |  |
| company_name | string | Y, if business = 1 |  |  |
| company_coc | string | Y, if business = 1 |  |  |
| company_vat | string | N |  | If business = 1, Required according to flow settings |
| contract_date | date | N |  | In DD-MM-YYYY format. If not empty, then signifies that it is a completed offer. |
| contract_pdf | array | N |  | Required if contract_date is not empty. Array expects 3 keys. First one is "content", which is the Base64 string of the contract pdf. Second one is "extension", which should always be 'pdf'. Third one is "created_at", which is the file created date time in "YYYY-mm-dd hh:mm:ss" format |
| signature | string | N |  | Required based on flow settings, Base64 encoded string of image |
| questionData | array | Y |  | Product question identifiers along with corresponding answers |
| agreements | array | Y |  | Agreement identifiers with answer as "1" for accepted agreements |
| related_products | array | Y |  | Array of list related product ids selected in the sale |
| initial_user_firstname | string | N |  | The first name of the user who did the sale |
| initial_user_lastname | string | N |  | The last name of the user who did the sale |
| initial_organisation_name | string | N |  | The name of the organisation which did the sale |
| initial_created_at | datetime | N |  | In YYYY-mm-dd hh:mm:ss format. The date and time on which the sale was originally created |
| external_reference | string | N |  | An order reference identifier that can be used by the API partners |
| relation_id | integer | N |  | The ID of the relation that needs to be linked to the sale. This has to be a valid relation ID that is accessible to the user. If relation_id is not sent, then a new relation will be created and linked to the sale. |

request.description:
Create order for telecom flow.

Replace {{flow_dentifier}} with the identifier of the flow.

Replace {{flow_type_identifier}} with the identifier of the flow type.

In the questionData object, the product question identifiers needed for the flow should be sent along with the corresponding answers

In the agreements object, the agreement identifiers needed for the flow should be sent along with value "1" for accepted agreements.

#### Completed and uncompleted orders

The API endpoint can be used to create both completed and uncompleted orders. Uncompleted orders result in a new contract pdf being generated and also a confirmation/verification email being sent to customers based on flow settings. A completed order will not send an email. An order via the API is considered as a completed order if it contains the "contract_date" parameter.

**Body Parameters**

| Field | Type | Mandatory | Possible Value(s) | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| transaction_type | string | Y | order |  |
| connection_postcode | string | Y |  | Connection postcode |
| connection_housenumber | integer | Y |  | Connection house number |
| connection_suffix | string | N |  | Connection house number extension |
| connection_streetname | string | Y |  | Connection street name |
| connection_city | string | Y |  | Connection city |
| postcode | string | Y |  | Contractant postcode |
| housenumber | string | Y |  | Correspondence house number |
| suffix | string | N |  | Correspondence house number extension |
| streetname | string | Y |  | Correspondence street name |
| city | string | Y |  | Correspondence city |
| gender | string | N |  |  |
| firstname | string | Y |  |  |
| lastname | string | Y |  |  |
| birthdate | string | Y |  | DOB in format dd-mm-yyyy |
| email | string | Y |  |  |
| phone | string | Y |  |  |
| iban | string | N |  | Required according to flow settings |
| iban_holder | string | N |  | Required according to flow settings |
| sale_channel | string | N | 'd2d', 'retail', 'telemarketing', 'online', 'f2f' |  |
| product_id | integer | Y |  | ID of product |
| business | integer | Y | 1, 0 | 1 = business, 0 = consumer |
| contact_person | string | Y, if business = 1 |  |  |
| company_name | string | Y, if business = 1 |  |  |
| company_coc | string | Y, if business = 1 |  |  |
| company_vat | string | N |  | If business = 1, Required according to flow settings |
| contract_date | date | N |  | In DD-MM-YYYY format. If not empty, then signifies that it is a completed order. |
| contract_pdf | array | N |  | Required if contract_date is not empty. Array expects 3 keys. First one is "content", which is the Base64 string of the contract pdf. Second one is "extension", which should always be 'pdf'. Third one is "created_at", which is the file created date time in "YYYY-mm-dd hh:mm:ss" format |
| signature | string | N |  | Required based on flow settings, Base64 encoded string of image |
| questionData | array | Y |  | Product question identifiers along with corresponding answers |
| agreements | array | Y |  | Agreement identifiers with answer as "1" for accepted agreements |
| related_products | array | Y |  | Array of list related product ids selected in the sale |
| initial_user_firstname | string | N |  | The first name of the user who did the sale |
| initial_user_lastname | string | N |  | The last name of the user who did the sale |
| initial_organisation_name | string | N |  | The name of the organisation which did the sale |
| initial_created_at | datetime | N |  | In YYYY-mm-dd hh:mm:ss format. The date and time on which the sale was originally created. If the sale is completed, then this date time (if not empty), is considered as the contract signed date. |
| external_reference | string | N |  | An order reference identifier that can be used by the API partners |
| relation_id | integer | N |  | The ID of the relation that needs to be linked to the sale. This has to be a valid relation ID that is accessible to the user. If relation_id is not sent, then a new relation will be created and linked to the sale. |

request.description:
This endpoint can be used to update the details of a sale already created. The endpoint is available for both admin and agent users.

This endpoint can currently be only used to edit sales of specific flow types and also sales that are not:

- cancelled
    
- sent
    
- anonymised
    
- locked
    
- Salesdock integration sales
    

The endpoint accepts mainly the following:

1. customer - This object contains all ther personal and address information of the customer.
    
2. product_questions - This object contains the product questions of the sale. A "delete" array passsed in this with product question identifiers, will remove existing product questions from the sale and an "upsert" array will insert/update product questions into the sale.
    
3. agreements - Agreemeent data need to be sent for orders. A "delete" array passsed in this with agreement idnetifiers, will remove existing agreements from the sale and an "upsert" array will insert/update agreements into the sale.
    
4. flow_telecom - This array will contain telecom flow specific information. Right now, it accepts one array called "products". These accept the add on products of the sale that can be modified. A sample json object os below
    

``` json
{
    "products": {
        "delete": [
                35,
                20
            ]
        },
        "upsert": [
                12,
                15
            ]
        }
    }
}

 ```

In the above request, the products in the delete array will be inserted and those in the upsert array will be updated or inserted.

request.description:
Hosted voice sales can be created using this endpoint if the Hosted voice app is installed in the account.

Replace {{flow_dentifier}} with the identifier of the flow.

Replace {{flow_type_identifier}} with the identifier of the flow type.

In the questionData object, the product question identifiers needed for the flow should be sent along with the corresponding answers

In the agreements object, the agreement identifiers needed for the flow should be sent along with value "1" for accepted agreements.

In the related_products object, the addon products can be sent.

#### Completed and uncompleted offers

The API endpoint can be used to create both completed and uncompleted offers. Uncompleted offers result in a new contract pdf being generated and an email being sent to the customer to accept the offer. A completed offer will not send an offer acceptance email. An offer via the API is considered as a completed offer if it contains the "contract_date" parameter.

#### Completed and uncompleted orders

The API endpoint can be used to create both completed and uncompleted orders. Uncompleted orders result in a new contract pdf being generated and also a confirmation/verification email being sent to customers based on flow settings. A completed order will not send an email. An order via the API is considered as a completed order if it contains the "contract_date" parameter.

**Body Parameters**

| Field | Type | Mandatory | Possible Value(s) | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| transaction_type | string | Y | offer, order |  |
| connection_postcode | string | Y |  | Connection postcode |
| connection_housenumber | integer | Y |  | Connection house number |
| connection_suffix | string | N |  | Connection house number extension |
| connection_streetname | string | Y |  | Connection street name |
| connection_city | string | Y |  | Connection city |
| postcode | string | Y |  | Correspondence postcode |
| housenumber | string | Y |  | Correspondence house number |
| suffix | string | Y |  | Correspondence house number extension |
| streetname | string | Y |  | Correspondence street name |
| city | string | Y |  | Correspondence city |
| gender | string | N |  |  |
| firstname | string | Y |  |  |
| lastname | string | Y |  |  |
| birthdate | string | Y |  | DOB in format dd-mm-yyyy |
| email | string | Y |  |  |
| phone | string | Y |  |  |
| iban | string | N |  | Required according to flow settings |
| iban_holder | string | N |  | Required according to flow settings |
| sale_channel | string | N | 'd2d', 'retail', 'telemarketing', 'online', 'f2f' |  |
| product_id | integer | Y |  | ID of product |
| business | integer | Y | 1, 0 | 1 = business, 0 = consumer |
| contact_person | string | Y, if business = 1 |  |  |
| company_name | string | Y, if business = 1 |  |  |
| company_coc | string | Y, if business = 1 |  |  |
| company_vat | string | N |  | If business = 1, Required according to flow settings |
| contract_date | date | N |  | In DD-MM-YYYY format. If not empty, then signifies that it is a completed offer. |
| contract_pdf | array | N |  | Required if contract_date is not empty. Array expects 3 keys. First one is "content", which is the Base64 string of the contract pdf. Second one is "extension", which should always be 'pdf'. Third one is "created_at", which is the file created date time in "YYYY-mm-dd hh:mm:ss" format |
| signature | string | N |  | Required based on flow settings, Base64 encoded string of image |
| questionData | array | Y |  | Product question identifiers along with corresponding answers |
| agreements | array | Y |  | Agreement identifiers with answer as "1" for accepted agreements |
| initial_user_firstname | string | N |  | The first name of the user who did the sale |
| initial_user_lastname | string | N |  | The last name of the user who did the sale |
| initial_organisation_name | string | N |  | The name of the organisation which did the sale |
| external_reference | string | N |  | An order reference identifier that can be used by the API partners |
| initial_created_at | datetime | N |  | In YYYY-mm-dd hh:mm:ss format. The date and time on which the sale was originally created |
| includes_internet | int | Y | 0,1 | 0 => Internet is not included; 1 => Internet is included |
| related_products | array | N |  | This object contains the details of the sub products (if any) that are being sold in this sale. |
| relation_id | integer | N |  | The ID of the relation that needs to be linked to the sale. This has to be a valid relation ID that is accessible to the user. If relation_id is not sent, then a new relation will be created and linked to the sale. |

request.description:
Solar panels sales can be created using this endpoint if the Solar panel voice app is installed in the account. Replace {{flow_dentifier}} with the identifier of the flow. In the questionData object, the product question identifiers needed for the flow should be sent along with the corresponding answers In the agreements object, the agreement identifiers needed for the flow should be sent along with value "1" for accepted agreements. #### Completed and uncompleted offers The API endpoint can be used to create both completed and uncompleted offers. Uncompleted offers result in a new contract pdf being generated and an email being sent to the customer to accept the offer. A completed offer will not send an offer acceptance email. An offer via the API is considered as a completed offer if it contains the "contract_date" parameter. #### Completed and uncompleted orders The API endpoint can be used to create both completed and uncompleted orders. Uncompleted orders result in a new contract pdf being generated and also a confirmation/verification email being sent to customers based on flow settings. A completed order will not send an email. An order via the API is considered as a completed order if it contains the "contract_date" parameter. **Body Parameters** | Field | Type | Mandatory | Possible Value(s) | Description | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | transaction_type | string | Y | offer, order | | | connection_postcode | string | Y | | Connection postcode | | connection_housenumber | integer | Y | | Connection house number | | connection_suffix | string | N | | Connection house number extension | | connection_streetname | string | Y | | Connection street name | | connection_city | string | Y | | Connection city | | postcode | string | Y | | Correspondence postcode | | housenumber | string | Y | | Correspondence house number | | suffix | string | Y | | Correspondence house number extension | | streetname | string | Y | | Correspondence street name | | city | string | Y | | Correspondence city | | gender | string | N | | | | firstname | string | Y | | | | lastname | string | Y | | | | birthdate | string | Y | | DOB in format dd-mm-yyyy | | email | string | Y | | | | phone | string | Y | | | | iban | string | N | | Required according to flow settings | | iban_holder | string | N | | Required according to flow settings | | sale_channel | string | N | 'd2d', 'retail', 'telemarketing', 'online', 'f2f' | | | product_id | integer | Y | | ID of package product or panel product | | business | integer | Y | 1, 0 | 1 = business, 0 = consumer | | contact_person | string | Y, if business = 1 | | | | company_name | string | Y, if business = 1 | | | | company_coc | string | Y, if business = 1 | | | | company_vat | string | N | | If business = 1, Required according to flow settings | | contract_date | date | N | | In DD-MM-YYYY format. If not empty, then signifies that it is a completed offer. | | contract_pdf | array | N | | Required if contract_date is not empty. Array expects 3 keys. First one is "content", which is the Base64 string of the contract pdf. Second one is "extension", which should always be 'pdf'. Third one is "created_at", which is the file created date time in "YYYY-mm-dd hh:mm:ss" format | | signature | string | N | | Required based on flow settings, Base64 encoded string of image | | questionData | array | Y | | Product question identifiers along with corresponding answers | | agreements | array | Y | | Agreement identifiers with answer as "1" for accepted agreements | | initial_user_firstname | string | N | | The first name of the user who did the sale | | initial_user_lastname | string | N | | The last name of the user who did the sale | | initial_organisation_name | string | N | | The name of the organisation which did the sale | | external_reference | string | N | | An order reference identifier that can be used by the API partners | | initial_created_at | datetime | N | | In YYYY-mm-dd hh:mm:ss format. The date and time on which the sale was originally created | | sell_type | string | Y | 'buy', 'rent' | | | usage | string | Y | | Electricity usage | | building_type | string | N | 'apartment', 'townhouse', 'corner_house', 'detached' | | | street_view_url | string | N | | Google street view URL | | panel_product | string | Y | | ID of panel product | | panel_count | int | Y | | Number of panels | | fuse_box_capacity | string | N | '1 x 25A', '3 x 25A', '1 x 35A', '3 x 35A' | | | roof_type | string | N | 'sloping', 'flat' | | | roofing | string | N | 'Roof tiles',
'Slates',
'Corrugated iron',
'Reed',
'Steel',
'Zinc',
'Green roof', 'Bitumen',
'EPDM',
'PVC',
'Green roof' | | | roof_direction | string | N | 'South', 'Southeast', 'Southwest', 'West', 'East' | | | wp_kwh_factor | string | N | | Solar panel power factor | | roof_length | string | N | | | | roof_width | string | N | | | | dynamic_pricing | string | N | '1', '0' | | | dynamic_product_name | string | N | | Required if dynamic_pricing = '1' | | dynamic_product_price | string | N | | Required if dynamic_pricing = '1' | | dynamic_product_price_vat_type | string | N | 'inclusive', 'exclusive' | Required if dynamic_pricing = '1' | | vat_lump_sum | string | N | 'include_in_totals', 'disclaimer_only' | | | option_products | array | N | | | | dynamic_options | array | N | | | | selected_options | array | N | | | | external_reference | string | N | | An order reference identifier that can be used by the API partners | | relation_id | integer | N | | The ID of the relation that needs to be linked to the sale. This has to be a valid relation ID that is accessible to the user. If relation_id is not sent, then a new relation will be created and linked to the sale. |

request.description:
This endpoint is used to retrieve the possible solar packages that the user has access to.

**Body Parameters**

| Field | Type | Mandatory | Possible Value(s) | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| number_of_panels | number | Y |  | Number of panels |
| usage_electricity | number | Y |  | Electricity usage |
| roof_type | string | Y | sloping, flat | Type of roof |
| roof_orientation | string | N | south, southeast, southwest, west, east, north, northeast, northwest | The direction where the roof is facing. Required when roof type is sloping. |
| powerfactor | number | N |  | Powerfactor is in KWh. Posible value is numeric and maximum value is 1. Required when roof orientation is not present and roof type is sloping. Empty value will treated as 0. |
| type | string | Y | buy, rent | Default value will be buy |
| solar_panel_id | number | N |  | Solar panel ID. Used to filter the solar packages. |
| return_tariff | number | N |  | Electricity return |
| energy_tariff | number | N |  | Electricity tariff. |
| customfield_filters | array | N |  | Array of custom fields with identifier and value (key value pair). Only the options with correct custom field values will be returned if this is sent |
| additional_costs | array | N |  | Array of additional costs |

For each package, apart from the basic information, the response also contains:

- package_content - The different products supported in the package
- pricing - Monthly and total prices in the package
- yield - The yield in KWH from the package
- savings - The return on investment in using the package

response.name: Solar compare

request.description:
Heat pumps sales can be created using this endpoint if the Heat pump app is installed in the account.

Replace {{flow_dentifier}} with the identifier of the flow.

In the questionData object, the product question identifiers needed for the flow should be sent along with the corresponding answers

In the agreements object, the agreement identifiers needed for the flow should be sent along with value "1" for accepted agreements.

#### Completed and uncompleted offers

The API endpoint can be used to create both completed and uncompleted offers. Uncompleted offers result in a new contract pdf being generated and an email being sent to the customer to accept the offer. A completed offer will not send an offer acceptance email. An offer via the API is considered as a completed offer if it contains the "contract_date" parameter.

#### Completed and uncompleted orders

The API endpoint can be used to create both completed and uncompleted orders. Uncompleted orders result in a new contract pdf being generated and also a confirmation/verification email being sent to customers based on flow settings. A completed order will not send an email. An order via the API is considered as a completed order if it contains the "contract_date" parameter.

**Body Parameters**

| Field | Type | Mandatory | Possible Value(s) | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| transaction_type | string | Y | offer, order |  |
| connection_postcode | string | Y |  | Connection postcode |
| connection_housenumber | integer | Y |  | Connection house number |
| connection_suffix | string | N |  | Connection house number extension |
| connection_streetname | string | Y |  | Connection street name |
| connection_city | string | Y |  | Connection city |
| postcode | string | Y |  | Correspondence postcode |
| housenumber | string | Y |  | Correspondence house number |
| suffix | string | Y |  | Correspondence house number extension |
| streetname | string | Y |  | Correspondence street name |
| city | string | Y |  | Correspondence city |
| gender | string | N |  |  |
| firstname | string | Y |  |  |
| lastname | string | Y |  |  |
| birthdate | string | Y |  | DOB in format dd-mm-yyyy |
| email | string | Y |  |  |
| phone | string | Y |  |  |
| iban | string | N |  | Required according to flow settings |
| iban_holder | string | N |  | Required according to flow settings |
| sale_channel | string | N | 'd2d', 'retail', 'telemarketing', 'online', 'f2f' |  |
| product_id | integer | Y |  | ID of package product or panel product |
| business | integer | Y | 1, 0 | 1 = business, 0 = consumer |
| contact_person | string | Y, if business = 1 |  |  |
| company_name | string | Y, if business = 1 |  |  |
| company_coc | string | Y, if business = 1 |  |  |
| company_vat | string | N |  | If business = 1, Required according to flow settings |
| contract_date | date | N |  | In DD-MM-YYYY format. If not empty, then signifies that it is a completed offer. |
| contract_pdf | array | N |  | Required if contract_date is not empty. Array expects 3 keys. First one is "content", which is the Base64 string of the contract pdf. Second one is "extension", which should always be 'pdf'. Third one is "created_at", which is the file created date time in "YYYY-mm-dd hh:mm:ss" format |
| signature | string | N |  | Required based on flow settings, Base64 encoded string of image |
| questionData | array | Y |  | Product question identifiers along with corresponding answers |
| agreements | array | Y |  | Agreement identifiers with answer as "1" for accepted agreements |
| initial_user_firstname | string | N |  | The first name of the user who did the sale |
| initial_user_lastname | string | N |  | The last name of the user who did the sale |
| initial_organisation_name | string | N |  | The name of the organisation which did the sale |
| external_reference | string | N |  | An order reference identifier that can be used by the API partners |
| initial_created_at | datetime | N |  | In YYYY-mm-dd hh:mm:ss format. The date and time on which the sale was originally created |
| sell_type | string | Y | 'buy', 'rent' |  |
| number_of_persons | number | Y |  | Nimber of people in the household |
| electricity_usage | number | Y |  | Usage of electricity per year in kWh |
| gas_usage | number | Y |  | Usage of electricity per year in m3 |
| heating_system | string | Y | 'underfloor-heating', 'underfloor-heating-and-radiators', 'radiators-and-underfloor-heating', 'radiators', 'convectors' |  |
| gas_usage_tap_water_per_person | number | Y |  | Gas usage tap water per person |
| external_reference | string | N |  | An order reference identifier that can be used by the API partners |
| relation_id | integer | N |  | The ID of the relation that needs to be linked to the sale. This has to be a valid relation ID that is accessible to the user. If relation_id is not sent, then a new relation will be created and linked to the sale. |

request.description:
This endpoint can be used to update the details of a sale already created. The endpoint is available for both admin and agent users.

This endpoint can currently be only used to edit sales of specific flow types and also sales that are not:

- cancelled
    
- sent
    
- anonymised
    
- locked
    
- Salesdock integration sales
    

The endpoint accepts mainly the following:

1. customer - This object contains all ther personal and address information of the customer.
    
2. product_questions - This object contains the product questions of the sale. A "delete" array passsed in this with product question identifiers, will remove existing product questions from the sale and an "upsert" array will insert/update product questions into the sale.
    
3. agreements - Agreemeent data need to be sent for orders. A "delete" array passsed in this with agreement idnetifiers, will remove existing agreements from the sale and an "upsert" array will insert/update agreements into the sale.
    
4. flow_heatpump - This array will contain the heapump flow specific information. It accepts one array called "products". These accept the add on products of the sale that can be modified. A sample json object os below
    

``` json
{
    "products": {
        "delete": [
                35,
                20
            ]
        },
        "upsert": [
                12,
                15
            ]
        }
    }
}

 ```

In the above request, the products in the delete array will be inserted and those in the upsert array will be updated or inserted.

Other than addon products, heatpump specific information as below are also accepted inside this object:

- number_of_persons
    
- electricity_usage
    
- gas_usage
    
- heating_system
    
- gas_usage_tap_water_per_person
    
- electricity_tariff
    
- electricity_return_tariff
    
- gas_tariff
    

The details of the data within these can be verified using the sample request

request.description:
Get details of a product using the product id. Replace {{product_id}} in the URL with the id of the product.

The product id can be obtained from the "Get products" API call.

Few important details returned in this response are:

- id - The id of the product. Used when creating sale
    
- type - Product type
    
- price - One time price
    
- price_action - One time promotion price
    
- price_monthly - Monthly price
    
- price_monthly_action - Monthly promotion price
    
- price_monthly_action_type - If value is monthly, then denotes that the product has a monthly promotion price
    
- price_monthly_action_value - Number of months for whic promotion is applicable
    
- supplier_id - Id of the supplier of the product
    
- supplier_name - Name of the supplier of the product
    
- customfields - Array of custom fields of the product and their values

response.name: Get product
