request.description:
Get the list of product questions along with basic details.

The id obtained from this call can be used to get more details about the product question in the "Get product question" API call.

Also the product question identifiers can be used in the "Create offer" and "Create order" API calls.

response.name: Get product questions

request.description:
Get detailed information about a product question using the product question id

The product question id is received from the "Get product questions" API call.

The identifier can be used in the "Create offer" and "Create order" API calls along with the necessary answers.

Some of the important information returned in the response of this API call are:

- identifier - The identifier of the product question, that can be used when creating a sale.
- input_type - The type of values that are supported by the product question. All the input types supprt text values except for "mc-multi", which support multiple array values
- options - Comma separated list of supported values
- extra - JSON array of product types that support this product question

response.name: Get product question

The agreement identifier needs to be sent along with value "1" for accepted agreements when creating sales.

query.description: Only available in account scope. If draft sales need to be sent back, then send value as 1

request.description:
Get the list of sales during a specific period using different filters. Supported filters are described below. The id in the response, can be used in the "Get sale" API call to get more comprehensive details about the sale. **Extensive results** If the user also likes to see more details about the sales per row, this can be achieved by passing extended=true, in the request. This will fetch much more comprehensive results per sale. **Filter Parameters** | Parameter Name | Possible Value(s) or Type | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | period_filter_on | 'created_date', 'updated_date', 'finalized_at', 'cancelled_date', 'sent_date', 'deleted_date' | The date field to filter on.
created_date - Sale created date.
updated_date - Sale last updated date.
finalized_at - Sale signed date.
cancelled_date - Sale cancelled date.
sent_date - Sale processed date.
deleted_date - Sale deleted date.

finalized_at filters on signed sales. For integration sales it will return the sales that are handed over in the selected time period, but the finalized_at time will be filled with the signed data | | period | 'today', 'yesterday', 'this_week', 'last_week', 'last_30_days', 'this_month', 'last_month', 'custom' | Possible periods to filter on | | period_start | Date in YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss format | If period is custom | | period_end | Date in YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss format | If period is custom | | type | 'sale', 'offer' | To filter direct sales or offers | | flow_id | integer | The id of the flow of the transaction | | supplier_id | integer | The id of the supplier of the product of the transaction | | statuses | array | The possible statuses can be retrieved using the "Get sale statuses list" endpoint Multiple statuses can be passed. Eg: statuses\[0\]=new&statuses\[1\]=sent | | offer_statuses | 'open', '1' (accepted), '0' (declined), '2' (expired) | The status of the offers of the transactions. Multiple offer statuses can be filtered. Eg. offer_statuses\[0\]=open&offer_statuses\[1\]=1 | | product_types | array | The type of the product of the transaction. Multiple product types can be passed like: product_types\[0\]=simple&product_types\[1\]=default | | sale_ids | array | The ids of the sales can be passed as an array to return specific sales like: sale_ids\[0\]=123456&sale_ids\[1\]=5555555 | | organisations | array | The ids of the organisations can be passed as an array to return sales of specific organisations like: organisations\[0\]=1&organisations\[1\]=2 | | extended | boolean | Send extended=true, to see more comprehensive results per sale |

request.description:
Get the details of a sale using the sale_id.

The sale id can be obtained from the "Get sales" API call.

The response retrieves comprehensive details about the sale including the following information:

**Basic sale details**

- id - Sale id
    
- type - If sale is offer or order
    
- offer_accepted - true if offer is accepted, else false
    
- offer_answered_at - Date time at which offer was answered.
    
- created_at - Date time at which sale was created
    
- updated_at - Date time at which sale was last updated
    
- finalized_at - Date time at which sale was signed. For integration sales it will return the sales that are handed over in the selected time period when using finalized_at filter, but the finalized_at time will be filled with the signed data
    
- channel - The sales channel used to create the sale
    

**Processed**

Array with details regarding sale sent details. If null, then sale is not processed as yet. Response values are:

- processed_at - Date time at which sale was sent
    
- outgoing_id - Unique id from the destination to which sale was sent
    
- outgoing_app - Identifier of the destination to which sale was sent
    

**Cancelled**

Array with details regarding sale cancellation details. If null, then sale is not cancelled as yet. Response values are:

- cancelled_at - Date time at which sale was cancelled
    
- reason - Cancelled remarks
    
- cancelled_by - User array with details of user who cancelled the sale
    

**Source**

Array with details of the dialer row source of the sale. null if sale was not created via dialer row. Response can contain:

- id - ID of the dialer row source
    
- label - Dialer row source
    

**Customer**

The basic details regarding the custom for whom the sale was done.

- gender - Gender of the customer
    
- firstname - Firstname of the customer
    
- lastname - Last name of the customer
    
- email - Customer's email
    
- phone - Customer's phone
    
- business - true if business sale and false if consumer sale
    
- company_name - Name of the company if business sale
    
- contact_person - Contact person of the company for business sale
    
- address - array containing details of the deliver address
    
- correspondence_address - array containing details of the correspondence address. If correspondence address is same as delivery address, then null is returned.
    

**Created by**

created_by returns an array with two sub arrays containing information about the entity that created the sale -

- user - array with id, username, firstname and lastname of the user who created the sale
    
- organisation - array with id, identifier and label of the organisation who created the sale
    

**Lead**

If sale was created from a lead, this will return an array with details regarding the lead. By default, the response will be null.

**Relation**

Following details of the relation of the sale is returned:

- id - ID of the relation
    
- label - Name of the relation
    

**Product**

Following details of the product of the sale is returned:

- id - ID of the product
    
- identifier - Product identifier
    
- label - Product name
    

**Supplier**

Following detals of the supplier of the product of the sale is returned:

- id - ID of the supplier
    
- identifier - Supplier identifier
    
- label - Supplier name
    

**Flow**

Following details of the flow of the sale is returned:

- id - ID of the flow
    
- identifier - Flow identifier
    
- label - Flow title
    
- type- Flow type
    

**Flow data**

flow_data is an array that contains specific information with regards to the sale. There are five items that are returned in this response:

- additional_pricing - Any additional prices added in the sale
    
- product_questions - The details of the product questions and answers of the sale
    
- agreements - The details of the agreements that are accepted and not accepted in the sale
    
- introduction - The PDF introductory notes added in the sale
    

In product_questions array, if the product question is of type file, then it will return an API URL to download the file. The structure of the response will look something like below:

``` json
{
    "id": 6682,
    "identifier": "file-question",
    "type": "file",
    "label": "File question",
    "value": [
        {
            "name": "sample.pdf",
            "extension": "pdf",
            "location": "https://app.salesdock.nl/api/testomgeving/v1/user/sales/396/question/6682/download/eyJpdiI6IlFpVU5nQmU2ekxpNXVQSERtTkppdmc9PSIsInZhbHVlIjoia2hZdnZJMzFkVnRjenF1UXptT0NNdz09IiwibWFjIjoiNjU4MzY1MjhjYjY1ZjAyM2M3NmI0ZGM2MjEzOGQyN2M0OTI5YjI4NjlhOTA3Yjk0YWIyOTlkMmM4MTAxMWJiYyIsInRhZyI6IiJ9"
        },
        {
            "name": "unnamed.jpg",
            "extension": "jpg",
            "location": "https://app.saesldock.nl/api/testomgeving/v1/user/sales/396/question/6682/download/eyJpdiI6IjNhTThyN0pCOGVPNG1IMGt1TjZ1cVE9PSIsInZhbHVlIjoiUVV5WWxJVXU2RWpUdk9xaWVHRzV3QT09IiwibWFjIjoiYWYzZGYyZjUzNjUyMGVlZmE0NjNmM2Q1MWM5ZmFiYjVkZDVhODQ0NTgzNmRiNGU0YTVhMGU0NmRlYjc1Mzg1MCIsInRhZyI6IiJ9"
        }
    ]
}

 ```

**Flow data (flow specific)**

flow_data_(flow_type_identifier} is an array that returns flow specific response for the sale. For eg: if the sale flow is solar panels, then the response will be flow_data_solar_panel. Below the details of the response for each supported flow type.

<u>Energy - flow_data_energie</u>

- multisite - true if the sale has multi site and false if the sale does not have multi-sites
    
- sites - an array for each of the site in the sale with the following information per site
    - usage_origin - If the usage is estimated using the estimation tool, then 'estimated' and if the usage is manually entered by the agent, then 'manual'
        
    - living_working_function - true if the site has a living function and false if it does not
        
    - bag_function - The bag function returned from the BAG API for the sale
        
    - address - array of information containing the connection address (postcode, housenumber, suffix, streetname and city)
        
    - electricity - array containing the following information of the site's eletricity data:
        - ean_code
            
        - double_meter - true if site has double meter or else false
            
        - usage_single - Single meter usage
            
        - usage_normal - Normal usage if double meter is selected
            
        - usage_low - Low usage if double meter is selected
            
        - return_single - Electricity return for single meter
            
        - return_normal - Normal return if double meter is selected
            
        - return_low - Low return if double meter is selected
            
        - connection - Electricity connection type (eg: 3x25A, 3x35A etc.)
            
        - sjv_e_single - SJV Single meter usage
            
        - sjv_e_high - SJV Normal usage if double meter is selected
            
        - sjv_e_low - SJV Low usage if double meter is selected
            
        - sjv_e_return - SJV Electricity return for single meter
            
        - sjv_e_return_high - SJV Normal return if double meter is selected
            
        - sjv_e_return_low - SJV Low return if double meter is selected
            
    - gas - array containing the following information of the site's gas data:
        - ean_code
            
        - usage - Gas usage for the site
            
        - connection - Gas connection type (Onbemeten, G10, G16 etc)
            
        - gasregion - Gas region (G1, G2 etc)
            
        - sjv_g - SJV Gas usage
            
    - estimated_cost - array containing the following estimated costs for the site:
        - yearly - Total yearly cost
            
        - monthly - Total monthly cost
            
        - yearly_prijsplafond - Total yearly cost with price cap
            
        - monthly_prijsplafond - Total monthly cost with price cap
            

<u>Solar panels (flow_data_solar_panels)</u>

- type - 'rent' or 'buy'
    
- is_package - true if a package is selected or else false
    
- package_content - Comma separated list of products in the package
    
- options - Comma separated list of options selected
    
- laying_plan - array with below information regarding the laying plan documents uploaded for the sale -
    - name - Document name
        
    - extension - Document extension
        
    - url - Link to download the document
        
- profile - array with following information about the sale:
    - usage_in_kwh - Estimate of usage
        
    - tariff_energy - Electricity tariff
        
    - tariff_energy_return - Electricity return tariff
        
    - fusebox_capacity - Capacity of the selected fuse box
        
- roof - array with following information regarding the roof
    - sections
        - number_of_panels - Number of panels needed
            
        - orientation - Roof orientation (north, south, east etc)
            
        - type - Roof type (flat or sloping)
            
        - material - Material used in roofing (Roof tiles, Slates, Steel, Zinc etc)
            
        - surface - Total roof area
            
- yield - array with yield information
    - yearly_in_kwh - Yearly yield in KWH
        
- savings - Array of savings after installation of solar panels
    - per_year - Total savings per year
        
    - roi_in_years - Return of investment after a period
        

<u>Heat pumps (</u>flow_data_heat_pump<u>)</u>

- type - rent or buy
    
- household_size - number of people in the household
    
- electricity_usage - Electricity usage in kWh
    
- electricity_tariff - Array of electricity tariff per kWh for aplicable ranges
    
- electricity_return_tariff - Electricity tariff per kWh for electricity produced
    
- gas_usage - Gas usage in m3
    
- gas_tariff - Gas tariff per m3
    
- dispensing_system - The heating system used (underfloor-heating, underfloor-heating-and-radiators, radiators-and-underfloor-heating, radiators and convectors)
    
- gas_consumption_tapwater_per_person - Gas for tap water per person
    

**Customfields**

extrafields, will return sale, product and supplier extra fields that are saved in the sale.

request.description:
Get all sales in the format of different export templates using different filters. The response format is dependent on the {{export_template}} parameter. Possible values for {{export_template}} include 'default', 'energie', 'telecom' etc. This endpoint is only available for admin users. **Filter Parameters** | Parameter Name | Possible Value(s) or Type | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | period_filter_on | 'created_date', 'updated_date', 'finalized_at', 'cancelled_date', 'sent_date', 'deleted_date' | The date field to filter on.
created_date - Sale created date.
updated_date - Sale last updated date.
finalized_at - Sale signed date.
cancelled_date - Sale cancelled date.
sent_date - Sale processed date.
deleted_date - Sale deleted date.
| | period | 'today', 'yesterday', 'this_week', 'last_week', 'last_30_days', 'this_month', 'last_month', 'custom' | Possible periods to filter on | | period_start | Date in YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss format | If period is custom | | period_end | Date in YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss format | If period is custom | | type | 'sale', 'offer' | To filter direct sales or offers | | flow_id | integer | The id of the flow of the transaction | | supplier_id | integer | The id of the supplier of the product of the transaction | | statuses | array | The possible statuses can be retrieved using the "Get sale statuses list" endpoint Multiple statuses can be passed. Eg: statuses\[0\]=new&statuses\[1\]=sent | | offer_statuses | 'open', '1' (accepted), '0' (declined), '2' (expired) | The status of the offers of the transactions. Multiple offer statuses can be filtered. Eg. offer_statuses\[0\]=open&offer_statuses\[1\]=1 | | product_types | array | The type of the product of the transaction. Multiple product types can be passed like: product_types\[0\]=simple&product_types\[1\]=default | | sale_ids | array | The ids of the sales can be passed as an array to return specific sales like: sale_ids\[0\]=123456&sale_ids\[1\]=5555555 |

response.name: Get energy transactions by export template

request.description:
Create sale (order or offer) for default flow.

Replace {{flow_dentifier}} with the identifier of the flow.

Replace {{flow_type_identifier}} with the identifier of the flow type.

In the questionData object, the product question identifiers needed for the flow should be sent along with the corresponding answers

In the agreements object, the agreement identifiers needed for the flow should be sent along with value "1" for accepted agreements.

#### Completed and uncompleted offers

The API endpoint can be used to create both completed and uncompleted offers. Uncompleted offers result in a new contract pdf being generated and an email being sent to the customer to accept the offer. A completed offer will not send an offer acceptance email. An offer via the API is considered as a completed offer if it contains the "contract_date" parameter.

#### Completed and uncompleted orders

The API endpoint can be used to create both completed and uncompleted orders. Uncompleted orders result in a new contract pdf being generated and also a confirmation/verification email being sent to customers based on flow settings. A completed order will not send an email. An order via the API is considered as a completed order if it contains the "contract_date" parameter.

**Body Parameters**

| Field | Type | Mandatory | Possible Value(s) | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| transaction_type | string | Y | offer, order |  |
| connection_postcode | string | Y |  | Connection postcode |
| connection_housenumber | integer | Y |  | Connection house number |
| connection_suffix | string | N |  | Connection house number extension |
| connection_streetname | string | Y |  | Connection street name |
| connection_city | string | Y |  | Connection city |
| postcode | string | Y |  | Contractant postcode |
| housenumber | string | Y |  | Correspondence house number |
| suffix | string | Y |  | Correspondence house number extension |
| streetname | string | Y |  | Correspondence street name |
| city | string | Y |  | Correspondence city |
| gender | string | N |  |  |
| firstname | string | Y |  |  |
| lastname | string | Y |  |  |
| birthdate | string | Y |  | DOB in format dd-mm-yyyy |
| email | string | Y |  |  |
| phone | string | Y |  |  |
| iban | string | N |  | Required according to flow settings |
| iban_holder | string | N |  | Required according to flow settings |
| sale_channel | string | N | 'd2d', 'retail', 'telemarketing', 'online', 'f2f' |  |
| product_id | integer | Y |  | ID of product |
| business | integer | Y | 1, 0 | 1 = business, 0 = consumer |
| contact_person | string | Y, if business = 1 |  |  |
| company_name | string | Y, if business = 1 |  |  |
| company_coc | string | Y, if business = 1 |  |  |
| company_vat | string | N |  | If business = 1, Required according to flow settings |
| contract_date | date | N |  | In DD-MM-YYYY format. If not empty, then signifies that it is a completed offer. |
| contract_pdf | array | N |  | Required if contract_date is not empty. Array expects 3 keys. First one is "content", which is the Base64 string of the contract pdf. Second one is "extension", which should always be 'pdf'. Third one is "created_at", which is the file created date time in "YYYY-mm-dd hh:mm:ss" format |
| signature | string | N |  | Required based on flow settings, Base64 encoded string of image |
| questionData | array | Y |  | Product question identifiers along with corresponding answers |
| agreements | array | Y |  | Agreement identifiers with answer as "1" for accepted agreements |
| initial_user_firstname | string | N |  | The first name of the user who did the sale |
| initial_user_lastname | string | N |  | The last name of the user who did the sale |
| initial_organisation_name | string | N |  | The name of the organisation which did the sale |
| initial_created_at | datetime | N |  | In YYYY-mm-dd hh:mm:ss format. The date and time on which the sale was originally created. If the sale is a completed direct order, then this date time (if not empty), is considered as the contract signed date. |
| external_reference | string | N |  | An order reference identifier that can be used by the API partners |
| relation_id | integer | N |  | The ID of the relation that needs to be linked to the sale. This has to be a valid relation ID that is accessible to the user. If relation_id is not sent, then a new relation will be created and linked to the sale. |

request.description:
Default flows (based on the flow settings), can accept multiple products. This request shows an example of how to create a default sale with multiple products using the API end point.

The sub-products can be sent in the array called related_products as shown in this request. The product id, quantity of products and the category of the product (section-identifier) has to be sent in the request.

Replace {{flow_dentifier}} with the identifier of the flow.

Replace {{flow_type_identifier}} with the identifier of the flow type.

In the questionData object, the product question identifiers needed for the flow should be sent along with the corresponding answers

In the agreements object, the agreement identifiers needed for the flow should be sent along with value "1" for accepted agreements.

#### Completed and uncompleted offers

The API endpoint can be used to create both completed and uncompleted offers. Uncompleted offers result in a new contract pdf being generated and an email being sent to the customer to accept the offer. A completed offer will not send an offer acceptance email. An offer via the API is considered as a completed offer if it contains the "contract_date" parameter.

#### Completed and uncompleted orders

The API endpoint can be used to create both completed and uncompleted orders. Uncompleted orders result in a new contract pdf being generated and also a confirmation/verification email being sent to customers based on flow settings. A completed order will not send an email. An order via the API is considered as a completed order if it contains the "contract_date" parameter.

**Body Parameters**

| Field | Type | Mandatory | Possible Value(s) | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| transaction_type | string | Y | offer, order |  |
| connection_postcode | string | Y |  | Connection postcode |
| connection_housenumber | integer | Y |  | Connection house number |
| connection_suffix | string | N |  | Connection house number extension |
| connection_streetname | string | Y |  | Connection street name |
| connection_city | string | Y |  | Connection city |
| postcode | string | Y |  | Contractant postcode |
| housenumber | string | Y |  | Contractant house number |
| suffix | string | Y |  | Contractant house number extension |
| streetname | string | Y |  | Contractant street name |
| city | string | Y |  | Contractant city |
| gender | string | N |  |  |
| firstname | string | Y |  |  |
| lastname | string | Y |  |  |
| birthdate | string | Y |  | DOB in format dd-mm-yyyy |
| email | string | Y |  |  |
| phone | string | Y |  |  |
| iban | string | N |  | Required according to flow settings |
| iban_holder | string | N |  | Required according to flow settings |
| sale_channel | string | N | 'd2d', 'retail', 'telemarketing', 'online', 'f2f' |  |
| product_id | integer | Y |  | ID of product |
| business | integer | Y | 1, 0 | 1 = business, 0 = consumer |
| contact_person | string | Y, if business = 1 |  |  |
| company_name | string | Y, if business = 1 |  |  |
| company_coc | string | Y, if business = 1 |  |  |
| company_vat | string | N |  | If business = 1, Required according to flow settings |
| contract_date | date | N |  | In DD-MM-YYYY format. If not empty, then signifies that it is a completed offer. |
| contract_pdf | array | N |  | Required if contract_date is not empty. Array expects 3 keys. First one is "content", which is the Base64 string of the contract pdf. Second one is "extension", which should always be 'pdf'. Third one is "created_at", which is the file created date time in "YYYY-mm-dd hh:mm:ss" format |
| signature | string | N |  | Required based on flow settings, Base64 encoded string of image |
| questionData | array | Y |  | Product question identifiers along with corresponding answers |
| agreements | array | Y |  | Agreement identifiers with answer as "1" for accepted agreements |
| initial_user_firstname | string | N |  | The first name of the user who did the sale |
| initial_user_lastname | string | N |  | The last name of the user who did the sale |
| initial_organisation_name | string | N |  | The name of the organisation which did the sale |
| external_reference | string | N |  | An order reference identifier that can be used by the API partners |
| initial_created_at | datetime | N |  | In YYYY-mm-dd hh:mm:ss format. The date and time on which the sale was originally created |
| related_products | array | N |  | This object contains the details of the sub products (if any) that are being sold in this sale. |
| relation_id | integer | N |  | The ID of the relation that needs to be linked to the sale. This has to be a valid relation ID that is accessible to the user. If relation_id is not sent, then a new relation will be created and linked to the sale. |

request.description:
The update sale status endpoint can be used to update the status of a sale.

The parameters that are needed for this are:

{{sale_id}} - The id of the sale

{{status_identifier}} - The identifier of the status to which the sale needs to be changed to

{{status_remarks}} - Remarks if any while changing the status (optional)

The sale can also be mark as processed, if mark_processed=1, is sent in the request. Mark as processed will only work if the sale is in a status that can be processed.

request.description:
This endpoint can be used to fetch all products information related to a sale.

Replace {{sale_id}} with the id of the sale in the URL.

The response contains mainly two objects:

1. products - This contains the different products sold in the sale. There are additional information like the type of product, quantity sold etc also available. For each product, there is also a price_data object. This contains all the pricing information of the product.
2. additional_costs - Any additional costs that are entered in the sale are shown in this object. An additional_cost can be of type 'monthly' or 'onetime'. If an additional_cost is monthly, then 'number_months' parameter specifies the number of months the price is applicable for.
3. discounts - Discounts that are applied during the sale.
4. totals - This will show the total amounts of the sale.

response.name: Get sale products

request.description:
This endpoint, available for only admin users, retrieves the documents that are associated with a sale. Replace {{sale_id}} in the URL with the ID of the sale.

The response structure contains, multiple arrays with the following parameters. Each array specifies one document.

- name - Name of the document with extension
- location - API URL to download the document
    

The following documents related to the sale are returned:

1. Sale contract PDFs
2. PDF attachments uploaded in the checkout page
3. Proxy forms linked to the sale.

request.description:
This API endpoint fetches all the historical changes done to a sale. This endpoint is only available for admin users using account scope.

In the request URL, replace {{sale_id}} with the id of the sale.

The response body can contain the following parameters:

- type (string) - The type of activity done on the sale. Some examples are - created, updated, changeddata, assigned, resolved, cancelled etc.
- modified_at (date time in the format yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss) - The time at which the history record was updated
- previous_status (string) - The status identifier of the sale at the time of the history update
- new_status (string) - The status identifier of the sale after the history update
- user (array) - User object with user id and name of the user who modified the sale
- type_data (array) - Miscellaneous information concerning the type of sale update

response.name: Get sale history

request.description:
A concept transaction is a temporary transaction that can be created using this API with the customer's basic information. The link in the response can then be clicked to open the Salesdock application and do a sale where all the information is already prefilled

Concept transactions are usually used via Dialer software.

The information that is accepted, when creating a concept transaction is shown in the request params below. Apart from the customer personal information, four other parameters can be sent.

- source - This is to identify the dialer row software source
- source_id - This is the unique id of the concept transaction at the source
- souce_version - If the dialer row changes their information, they can update the source_version number so that Salesdock know what to expect
- source_metadata - This is an array of data that is unique to the dialer row.
    

If sale extra fields need to be also sent via the API, it can be sent in the format cf_{identifier}, where identifier is the identifier of the extra field.

request.description:
A concept transaction is a temporary transaction that can be created using this API with the customer's basic information. The link in the response can then be clicked to open the Salesdock application and do a sale where all the information is already prefilled

Concept transactions are usually used via Dialer software.

The information that is accepted, when creating a concept transaction is shown in the request params below. Apart from the customer personal information, four other parameters can be sent.

- source - This is to identify the dialer row software source
    
- source_id - This is the unique id of the concept transaction at the source
    
- souce_version - If the dialer row changes their information, they can update the source_version number so that Salesdock know what to expect
    
- source_metadata - This is an array of data that is unique to the dialer row.
    

If sale extra fields need to be also sent via the API, it can be sent in the format cf_{identifier}, where identifier is the identifier of the extra field.

This endpoint can also be used to send optin information that can then be attached to the sale created from the concept transaction. In order to create an opt-in, send a parameter "optin", with value 1.

An opt-in can have multiple channels. Each channel should be of type 'email' or 'phone'. Atleast one channel is mandatory in order to create an opt-in

request.description:
This endpoint helps retrieve the list of leads using different search parameters

Apart from the lead basic information, the following extra information regarding a lead is also returned in this response:

- extrafields - The values for extra fields of the lead
    
- questions - The lead questions and answers
    
- labels - The labels attached to the lead
    
- history - The historical changes done to the lead
    

**Sorting**

- The default sort order is leads.id in descending order
    
- If period_filter_on, is used in the filters, then sorting is done based on the period filter in descending order
    
- Override sorting, by passing sort_by in the parameters
    

The different options for sort_by are:

1. id_desc - Recently created
    
2. updated_at_desc - Recently updated
    
3. postcode_asc - Acending order of postcodes
    
4. postcode_desc - Descending order of postcodes
    
5. city_asc - Acending order of city name
    
6. city_desc - Descending order of city name
    
7. postcode_city_asc - Acending order of city name with postcode
    
8. postcode_city_desc - Descending order of city name with postcode
    
9. planned_date_asc - Planned date ascending order
    
10. planned_date_desc - Recently planned

response.name: Get leads

request.description:
This endpoint fetches the details of a lead using the lead id.

In the request URL, replace {{lead_id}} with the id of the lead.

Apart from the lead basic information, the following extra information regarding a lead is also returned in this response:

- extrafields - The values for extra fields of the lead
    
- questions - The lead questions and answers
    
- labels - The labels attached to the lead
    
- history - The historical changes done in the lead
    
- form - If a lead was created using a lead form, then the lead form elements and values will be in this object.
    

Each of the above objects will be in the following format

``` json
{
    "identifier": {
        "label": "",
        "type": "",
        "value": ""
    }
}

 ```

Where:

- identifier => Is the identifier of the extrafield, lead question or lead form element
    
- label => Is the label of the extrafield, lead question or lead form element
    
- type => Is the type of the extrafield, lead question or lead form element. It can be - text, list, signature, file
    
- value => Is the answer that is provided in the lead for the element
    

Inside form, if type is of file, then an API call will needed to be sent to fetch the file.

For file and list types, the value will be an array of answers

response.name: Get lead

request.description:
The "Create lead" endpoint can be used by admin users to create leads.

The basic information of the leads, lead questions and lead extra fields can be sent in this request.

Lead questions need to be sent in the format question_{{identifier}} (where identifier is the identifier of the lead question)

Extra fields need to be sent using the format cf_{{identifier}} (where identifier is the identifier of the custom field)

The assignee field can have values 'organisation' or 'user'.

If assigning lead to organisation, then organisation field is mandatory with organisation_id.

If assigning lead to user, then user field is mandatory with user_id.

**Body Parameters**

| Field | Type | Mandatory | Possible Value(s) | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| activity | string | N |  | Identifier of the lead activity |
| gender | string | N | 'male','female' | Gender of the lead |
| firstname | string | N |  | First name of the lead |
| lastname | string | N |  | Last name of the lead |
| postcode | string | N |  | Postcode of the lead. Required if the lead activity is type of D2D |
| housenumber | string | N |  | House number of the lead. Required if the lead activity is type of D2D |
| suffix | string | N |  | House number extension of the lead |
| streetname | string | N |  | Street name of the lead. Required if the lead activity is type of D2D |
| city | string | N |  | City of the lead. Required if the lead activity is type of D2D |
| birthdate | date | N |  | Date of birth of the lead. In d-m-Y format |
| email | string | N |  | Email of the lead |
| phone | string | N |  | Phone of the lead |
| business | string | Y | '1', '0' | 1 = Business; 0 = Consumer |
| company_name | string | N |  | Company name of business lead. Required if business = '1' |
| contact_person | string | N |  | Contact person of business lead |
| coc | string | N |  | KvK number of business lead |
| vat | string | N |  | Btw number of business lead |
| assign | string | Y | 'user', 'team', 'organisation' | If the lead needs to be assigned to a user, team or organisation |
| user | string | N |  | User id of assigned user. Required if the lead is assigned to a user |
| organisation_id | string | N |  | Organisation id of assigned organisation. Required if the lead is assigned to an organisation |
| team_id | string | N |  | Team id of assigned team. Required if the team is assigned to a team |
| planned_for_date | string | N | '1', '0' | If the lead needs to be planned for a date. 1 implies that the lead need to be planned for a date. This is required if assign=user |
| planned_date | date | N |  | Date for which lead is planned. Reqiured if planned_for_date = 1. In d-m-Y format |
| start_at | time | N |  | Starting time for which lead is planned. In hh:mm format |
| end_at | time | N |  | Ending time for which lead is planned. In hh:mm format |
| lead_source_id | string | N |  | The id of the lead source in Salesdock |
| lead_value | number | N |  | The value of the lead |

request.description:
The "Update lead" endpoint can be used by admin users to update leads. The lead_id needs to be passed in the URL in order to update this.

The basic information of the leads, lead questions and lead extra fields can be sent in this request.

Lead questions need to be sent in the format question_{{identifier}} (where identifier is the identifier of the lead question)

Extra fields need to be sent using the format cf_{{identifier}} (where identifier is the identifier of the custom field)

The assignee field can have values 'organisation' or 'user'.

If assigning lead to organisation, then organisation field is mandatory with organisation_id.

If assigning lead to user, then user field is mandatory with user_id.

**Body Parameters**

| Field | Type | Mandatory | Possible Value(s) | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| activity | string | N |  | Identifier of the lead activity |
| gender | string | N | 'male','female' | Gender of the lead |
| firstname | string | N |  | First name of the lead |
| lastname | string | N |  | Last name of the lead |
| postcode | string | N |  | Postcode of the lead. Required if the lead activity is type of D2D |
| housenumber | string | N |  | House number of the lead. Required if the lead activity is type of D2D |
| suffix | string | N |  | House number extension of the lead |
| streetname | string | N |  | Street name of the lead. Required if the lead activity is type of D2D |
| city | string | N |  | City of the lead. Required if the lead activity is type of D2D |
| birthdate | date | N |  | Date of birth of the lead. In d-m-Y format |
| email | string | N |  | Email of the lead |
| phone | string | N |  | Phone of the lead |
| business | string | Y | '1', '0' | 1 = Business; 0 = Consumer |
| company_name | string | N |  | Company name of business lead. Required if business = '1' |
| contact_person | string | N |  | Contact person of business lead |
| coc | string | N |  | KvK number of business lead |
| vat | string | N |  | Btw number of business lead |
| assign | string | Y | 'user', 'team', 'organisation' | If the lead needs to be assigned to a user, team or organisation |
| user | string | N |  | User id of assigned user. Required if the lead is assigned to a user |
| organisation_id | string | N |  | Organisation id of assigned organisation. Required if the lead is assigned to an organisation |
| team_id | string | N |  | Team id of assigned team. Required if the team is assigned to a team |
| planned_for_date | string | N | '1', '0' | If the lead needs to be planned for a date. 1 implies that the lead need to be planned for a date. This is required if assign=user |
| planned_date | date | N |  | Date for which lead is planned. Reqiured if planned_for_date = 1. In d-m-Y format |
| start_at | time | N |  | Starting time for which lead is planned. In hh:mm format |
| end_at | time | N |  | Ending time for which lead is planned. In hh:mm format |
| lead_source_id | string | N |  | The id of the lead source in Salesdock |
| relation_id | int | N |  | If the lead needs to be linked to a relation. To unlink a relation, pass the value as null |
| lead_value | number | N |  | Value of the lead |

request.description:
This endpoint fetches all the historical changes done to a lead.

In the request URL, replace {{lead_id}} with the id of the lead.

The response body can contain the following parameters:

- type (string) - The type of activity done on the lead. Some examples are - planned, updated, salecreated, offercreated, changeddata, deleted etc.
- modified_at (date time in the format yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss) - The time at which the history record was updated
- previous_status (string) - The status identifier of the lead at the time of the history update
- new_status (string) - The status identifier of the lead after the lead history update
- user (array) - User object with user id and name of the user who modified the lead
- type_data (array) - Miscellaneous information concerning the type of lead update

response.name: Get lead history

request.description:
This endpoint fetches all the status updates that were done for a lead by agents.

In the request URL, replace {{lead_id}} with the id of the lead.

Each result of a lead contains the following parameters:

- created_at (date time in the format yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss) - The time of status change
    
- action - vist or call
    
- previous_status (string) - The status identifier of the lead at the time of the history update
    
- new_status (string) - The status identifier of the lead after the lead history update
    
- contact_established - boolean based on the status settings
    
- sales_opportunity - boolean based on the status settings
    
- not_reached - boolean based on the status settings
    
- appointment - boolean based on the status settings
    
- latitude - latitude at which the result was don
    
- longitude - longitude at which the result was done
    
- distance - distance covered during the lead result
    
- user (object) - User object with user id, and name of the user who modified the lead
    
- organisation (object) - Organisation object with id, identifier and name of the user's organisation who modified the lead
    
- team (object) - Team object with id, identifier and name of the user's team who modified the lead
    
- township (object) - Township object with id, identifier and name of the townhsip of lead that instance

response.name: Get lead results by lead

Each result of a lead result contains the following parameters:

- created_at (date time in the format yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss) - The time of status change
    
- action - vist or call
    
- previous_status (string) - The status identifier of the lead at the time of the history update
    
- new_status (string) - The status identifier of the lead after the lead history update
    
- contact_established - boolean based on the status settings
    
- sales_opportunity - boolean based on the status settings
    
- not_reached - boolean based on the status settings
    
- appointment - boolean based on the status settings
    
- latitude - latitude at which the result was don
    
- longitude - longitude at which the result was done
    
- distance - distance covered during the lead result
    
- user (object) - User object with user id, and name of the user who modified the lead
    
- organisation (object) - Organisation object with id, identifier and name of the user's organisation who modified the lead
    
- team (object) - Team object with id, identifier and name of the user's team who modified the lead
    
- township (object) - Township object with id, identifier and name of the townhsip of lead that instance
    
- lead (object) - Contains the id of the lead

response.name: Get lead results

request.description:
This endpoint can be used to create a lead in Salesdock using a lead form template. The main parameters that are supported in this are:

- questionData - This is an array with the form element identifier as the key and the answer provided by the client as the value
- client_name - The name of the client if the lead form can also be completed by the client
- client_email - The email of the client if the lead form can also be completed by the client
- client_phone - The phone number of the client if the lead form can also be completed by the client
    

If the client needs to complete the form, then as soon as the form is saved then an email will be sent to the client to complete the form.

The response contains the lead id of the lead created.

request.description:
This endpoint fetches all the details of a form instance using the form instance ID.

The response contains an array called question-data, that contains the different elements in the form along with the answers.

If the form element, is of type file, then it will have a location paramenter, that points to a url from which the file can be downloaded.

response.name: Get form instance

request.description:
The fill form instance endpoint helps in creating new form instance for the form_id mentioned in the URL. The form elements can be sent as JSON in the request body.

The main parameters that are accepted in this request are:

- questionData - Array of all form elements along with answers. So the key should be the identifier of the form element, where as the value should be the answer
- client_name - Name of client, if supported in the form
- client_email - Email of the client, if supported in the form
- client_phone - Phone of the client, if supported in the form

query.description: Free text to search on the following parameters of a relation - name, company_name, email, postcode and email
The date period to filter on. Possible values - created_date, updated_date, deleted_date
The period to filter on. Possible values - custom, today, yesterday, this_week, last_week, last_30_days, this_month, last_month, this_year
Start date (if period is custom)
End date (if period is custom)
1=Business relations, 0=Consumer relations

request.description:
This endpoint fetches all the relations that are accessible to the user.

Apart from the personal/business information of the relation, the response also contains:

1. shared_with - Possible values are account, organisation and private to denote how the relation is shared.
2. organisation_id - The ID of the organisation to which the relation belongs.
    

Different filters, as mentioned below in the Query Params, can be used to filter for relations.

response.name: Get relations

request.description:
This endpoint fetches the details of a relation using the relation_id. Replace relation_id in the URL with the ID of the relation retrieved via the Get relations API call.

This resource is only available for account scope.

Apart from the basic relation details, two sub arrays are also returned:

1. customer -This contains all personal/business information of the relation
2. extrafields - This contains the extra field values of the relation. If the relation does not have any extra field values, this will return null.
    

The response also contains:

- shared_with - Possible values are account, organisation and private to denote how the relation is shared.
- organisation_id - The ID of the organisation to which the relation belongs.

response.name: Get relation

request.description:
This endpoint can be used to create a relation.

Relation extra fields can be sent in the format cf_{identifier} where identifier is the identifier of the extra field.

The other parameters that are supported are:

| Field | Type | Mandatory | Supported Value(s) | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| visibility | string | Y | private, organisation, account | The visibility of the relation. private is available for the user only. organisation is available for the organisation. account is available for the whole account. |
| user_id | integer | N |  | To make relations private to user |
| organisation_id | integer | N |  | To make relations private to organisation |
| business | string | Y | 0,1 | 0=consumer relation 1=business relation |
| postcode | string | N |  | Postcode of the relation |
| housenumber | string | N |  | House number of the relation |
| suffix | string | N |  | House number extension of the relation |
| streetname | string | N |  | Street name of the relation |
| city | string | N |  | City of the relation |
| gender | string | N |  |  |
| firstname | string | N |  | Required if business = 0 |
| lastname | string | N |  | Required if business = 0 |
| birthdate | string | N |  | Date of birth in format dd-mm-yyyy |
| email | string | N |  |  |
| phone | string | N |  |  |
| contact_person | string | N |  |  |
| company_name | string | N |  | Company name, required if business = 1 |
| company_coc | string | N |  | Chamber of commerice number for business relations |
| company_vat | string | N |  | BTW number for business relations |

request.description:
This endpoint can be used to update a relation. The ID of the relation needs to be passed in the URL.

This endpoint is only available for account scope users.

Relation extra fields can be sent in the format cf_{identifier} where identifier is the identifier of the extra field.

The other parameters that are supported are:

| Field | Type | Mandatory | Supported Value(s) | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| visibility | string | Y | private, organisation, account | The visibility of the relation. private is available for the user only. organisation is available for the organisation. account is available for the whole account. |
| user_id | int | N |  |  |
| organisation_id | int | N |  |  |
| business | string | Y | 0,1 | 0=consumer relation 1=business relation |
| postcode | string | N |  | Postcode of the relation |
| housenumber | string | N |  | House number of the relation |
| suffix | string | N |  | House number extension of the relation |
| streetname | string | N |  | Street name of the relation |
| city | string | N |  | City of the relation |
| gender | string | N |  |  |
| firstname | string | N |  | Required if business = 0 |
| lastname | string | N |  | Required if business = 0 |
| birthdate | string | N |  | Date of birth in format dd-mm-yyyy |
| email | string | N |  |  |
| phone | string | N |  |  |
| contact_person | string | N |  | Contact person of the company |
| company_name | string | N |  | Company name, required if business = 1 |
| company_coc | string | N |  | Chamber of commerice number for business relations |

request.description:
Get details of a product using the product id. Replace {{product_id}} in the URL with the id of the product.

The product id can be obtained from the "Get products" API call.

Few important details returned in this response are:

- id - The id of the product. Used when creating sale
    
- type - Product type
    
- price - One time price
    
- price_action - One time promotion price
    
- price_monthly - Monthly price
    
- price_monthly_action - Monthly promotion price
    
- price_monthly_action_type - If value is monthly, then denotes that the product has a monthly promotion price
    
- price_monthly_action_value - Number of months for whic promotion is applicable
    
- supplier_id - Id of the supplier of the product
    
- supplier_name - Name of the supplier of the product
    
- customfields - Array of custom fields of the product and their values

response.name: Get product

formdata.description: Name of the product
Unique slug identifier for the product
The id of the supplier.
String containing the the USP of the product. This will be shown in the agent portal when doing a sale.
Contract duration of the product in months
Whether product is busines (1) or consumer (0) or both (2). This is only for main product.
If only for new customers (0), existing customers (1) or for both(2).
1 = active, 0 = inactive
One time price
One time promotion price
Monthly price
Monthly promotion price
Number of months for promotion price
Start date from which product can be sold
End date till which product can be sold
Base64 string of the product image
Extension of the product image. Possible values - png.gif,jpg,jpeg
Product documents to be updated( add, delete, update) can be send as array value

request.description:
This endpoint can be used to connect different products to an organisation so that the users of the organisation can sell those products.

The organisation_id needs to be passed in the URL.

products array containing list of product_ids to connect to the organisation should be passed in the request body. If products array is not sent, then all available products are connected. If empty products array is sent, then all products are disconnected. The list of products connected are returned in the response.

This endpoint is available only for admin users.

organisations array containing list of organisation_ids to connect to the product should be passed in the request body. The filters that can be used to retrieve the tasks are explained in the params list below.

response.name: Get tasks

The user id of the assignd user of the task

item.name: Webhooks API

request.description: This endpoint lists the events that are currently supported in Salesdock webhooks. Users can use this list to subscribe to the events that they want to create a webhook for.

request.description:
This endpoint can be used to subscribe to a webhook event on Salesdock. The request supports two paraemters in the request body:

- event - This is the name of the event that the user would like to subscribe to. The event name can be received from the Get events API call
- target_url - This is the target URL that the webhook has to call when the event is executed on Salesdock
    

The response contains a parameter called uuid that can be used to unsubscribe from the event in future.

item.description:
Salesdock webhooks are used to notify users application that an event has occured in Salesdock. Technically these are user-defined [HTTP](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hypertext_Transfer_Protocol) callbacks. They are triggered by some event in a web application and can facilitate integrating different applications or third-party APIs.

The webhook API endpoints are only available for admin users using account scope.

**When are webhooks useful?**

Webhooks are very useful when a system wants to be notified as soon as something has occured on Salesdock app.

A real world example could be an instance in which, the user wants to be notified via Zapier when a customer accepts an offer on Salesdock.

**What does it mean to subscribe to a Webhook event?**

Salesdock application has created some events to which third party applications or APIs can subscribe. When that particular event happens in Salesdock, a webhook call is triggered and corresponding data is sent to a URL added in the subscription.

_Please contact Salesdock support if you would like to start using the Webhooks_

request.description:

The example shows a validation error that occurs when creating a lead.
